Please ask questions!

Professor Matthews was not only clear and concise with lessons, but he also made sure that you understood what he was teaching you. If you didnt understand and didnt ask then thats on you. His reviews for midterms and finals were spot on and he set you up for success. Easy A but it depends on your participation. ASK QUESTIONS!

So easy going, fun and treats you more like a peer than a student.  If you can't learn from him, you may want to rethink college.  It doesn't get any better than Matthews.

Professor Matthews is one of the best professors I've had till now.He is very enthusiastic about math, very interesting, and fun. His lectures aren't boring at all. I took precalc (math150) in HS, so this class was a piece of cake for me, didnt study at all and got a high A in the class. He's very helpful, and understanding.HIGHLY recommended!!

Very engaging and interesting.  He will challenge you to think of ways to relate methods to young learners.  Very available to help if you need it.  Go to class and participate.  Do the homework and you should do well.
Prof. Matthews is one of the best math teachers I've ever had.  He is always willing to help.  Doing the homework and the practice midterms make a HUGE difference.  Don't be afraid to ask questions.
awesome
OMG!! I LOVE THIS PROFESSOR!! He teaches very well and clearly. That's why I'm taking his math 132 class next. But I'm worning.... I like math. People who don't like math can be hard.

I had this man for high school math and wish he was teaching at Main campus for my math class because he's the only reason I understand math!  He's always willing to help, he LOVES what he does and always will.

Adam Matthews is the best math professor any of you will ever have. Why? Because he cares. He cares about each and everyone of his students and is extremely easy to talk to and his very helpful when you just don't understand what's going on. I love this guy and you will too!

From elementary school through middle school, high school, and even my first year of college, I can confidently say she was the worst professor I've ever had. She would abruptly move on from important topics, frequently derail the class with irrelevant tangents, and include material on exams that we never covered. I would not recommend her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors I have ever had in my collegiate career. She is a nice woman who can not teach whatsoever. Missing one class is practically a death sentence. She teaches everything BUT what will be on the test. If you want to stay up and study for a test of hers you might as well just sleep none of your notes will have her answers in it
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Overall she is a very nice lady. But her test are very hard and she doesn't  precent her lectures in class and on video like that. Therefore if I would have known this I would have dropped her class. She needs change the way she does things in my opinion. I will never take a class under her again

Eminem of the professors. Gives hard homework assignments and even harder tests. Absolutely no sympathy or emotion and regularly puts things on the test she never covered in class. When she talks about topics, she forgets to mention the parts she tests about.

had her twice and ended up dropping her class both times. it's very hard to get a good grade in this class if you are not a good test taker. she has an exam every 2-3.5 weeks and uses pop quizzes. nice lady but definitely needs to loosen up more

Very nice professor, however the tests were almost always a lot more difficult and in depth than the lectures were. The material didn't always line up. The lectures were not bad, just be prepared for difficult tests.

Dr. Bailey was fun to have as a professor with her purposely bad jokes that she would tell but that is pretty much the one positive thing that I can say about her class. She goes through slides pretty quickly and does not explain the material very well and she never gave out a study guide for any of the tests which made preparing for them hard.

Dr. Bailey simply doesn't seem to care that much about teaching. Tests make up the majority of your grade, and feature questions unrelated to the associated readings and material. Attendance is mandatory, and the class time is spent reading the same notes as recorded lectures. Material is pulled straight from text book. Take with someone else
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Tries to make class interesting, but it didn't work for me. Makes us read & study the textbook even though only 10% of Qs come exclusively from it. Always 1-5 Qs (out of 50) on the test that are either hardly taught, stray too far from material, or are hardly related to psych. However, is flexible with tests and course b/c understands the struggle.
Great. Easy class as long as you show up, pay attention, and read the assigned chapters. As long as you do what you are supposed to do there should be no problem getting an A.
Good professor. Only tests material given and offers extra credit on every exam. A few pop quizzes throughout the course, but not hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Even though she tries her hardest to keep lectures interesting with her upbeat personality, it often feels as if they are dragging on. She uses lots of pop quizzes to dissuade students from missing class. Reading the textbook is a must, as the four main tests are challenging. The massive amount of extra credit make up for the difficulty though.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Dr. Bailey is a good lecturer, however be ready to use the book for areas you may have missed in lecture because she goes quick. There is usually a pop quiz in between each test, so be prepared. In class participation is usually interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Definitely not my most favorite professor, she can be disrespectful to you in class which is very off putting and if you're anything like me it can ruin the class experience. Take this class online or with another psych professor as it's not worth the annoyance, disrespect, and unreasonable difficulty for an intro class.

reviews.comment: She was very disrespectful with me! I don't recommend taking psych with her.

reviews.comment: She gives awesome lectures that are easy to follow and explains everything in terms you can understand. Unlike other professors she gives good feedback and welcomes questions. But don't bring up stupid things just to hear yourself talk, it annoys everyone.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I always enjoy Dr. Bailey's classes, but don't expect an A if you don't put in the effort. Go to class. She gives you an outline for the notes, but you have to fill the info in. Read the textbook. It will help you understand any gray area and it follows along with the notes fairly well. She will take test questions from the book.
She was horrible! Keep talking! Going to fast! You have to write everything she say!

If you take notes and show up for class, you'll at least get a B. She does mostly multiple choice tests and one or two longer assignments per semester, with a few pop quizzes sprinkled in. I highly suggest taking her class. You wont get any crazy long papers or ridiculous tests.

She's a nice person, but do not recommend her as a teacher. She makes her tests much harder than what they need to be, expects you to know every page of the textbook by heart and does not provide study guides for the tests. I say avoid having a class with her as I never got anything below a B on a test for any pysch class before having her.

Extremely good professor! I took more notes than I did in any other class but she went over lectures in more detail. She gave examples of everything to make sure we understood and was very willing to answer questions. She lets you dp your lowest tet and lowest grade. I liked this class a lot!
You have to be there everyday foe her class. Her lectures are long and boring and she talks about her 18year old son as an example a lot in class

This was my easiest class this quarter.  She drops one quiz grade and one test grade lets you turn in rough drafts early so she can check them and gives extra credit.If you put in any effort at all you should get a decent grade actually try and you will get an A.I enjoyed this class a great deal I dont think she got off topic just gave examaples.
She only uses powerpoint.  Her tests are tough but if you study you will be fine.
Best teacher, couldn't ask for better. 4 exams get to drop 1. 4 quizes get to drop 1. Extra credits, and bonuses on Exams. If you study enough, its an easy A.

I wish she didnt just give out guidelines of her notes because it was hard to organize the material during her lectures.  She rambled a lot.  BUT HER TESTS ARE SO HARD, so be prepared and know the material, even then though, she still re-words things completely different in the test than the notes!  But she grades easily on homework and helps a lot

The subject matter was very interesting and she is very knowledgeable. However, her tests and assignments are meticulous and a little too much for a 100-level course. She tends to get off topic a lot and it seems like she talks more about her kids and family than psychology.
No Comments
I liked Fohl-Bailey; she was very helpful and easy to learn from.  She was more than willing to help you with any questions you have.  I recommend this teacher!
The class is very long, tests are hard, but she is very helpful and almost the entire course is on powerpoints-which are available on carmen. I learned a lot and ended up loving the class!
Good teacher and always around to help.
Very straightforward with what she expects of you, very clear, tests are a little hard.
ahh i dont know what is with everyone elses comments but i hate this class.. and she jus annoys me with her fanny pack and the extremely large glasses from 20 years ago... i DO NOT like this class
Easy class as long as you pay attention, journals can be a stumbling block however.
Long winded professor, but knows all her stuff!
Good prof.  She knows the stuff and is enthusiastic.
Not too much assignment and decent amount of readings each week. Give out study guide and use the same questions for the test. But the class is lectures heavy and boring, quite some group discussion.

reviews.comment: Amazing professor! I learned a lot in his class and it was pretty straightforward. He gave great real life examples of the concepts he taught. His tests are all paper and pencil paragraphs but he gives a study guide before the test and if you study it, you can easily get a good grade. Would definitely recommend!

Dr. Blanks was a wonderful professor. He wasn't very strict on grading and did a really nice job making you think critically. This class is more about applying texts and understand then completely memorizing. He was also accessible and was willing to work with you (even if you just simply forgot to do the homework). Exam heavy, talk in class.

Overall the class was pretty easy. You have a 10 point online discussion every week, two papers throughout the semester, two "midterms" (basically a timed, online discussion), and a final. As long as you read the readings you will get an A. Pretty easy grader and will respond to your emails within a couple hours at the latest.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: His lectures are really clear, which is good because he's a stickler for the details on the papers and on exams. If you show up to class and do the readings you should have no problems getting an A.
Doesn't let his bias get in the way. Loves to interact with students and is passionate about philosophy and religious studies. Pretty cool guy and a really fair professor.

Best math prof I've ever had BY FAR. He explains everything in detail and makes sure everyone understands what he's teaching before moving onto a different topic. He usually let us pick the dates for our quizzes. Lots of homework, but it's not hard as long as you paid attention and took notes during class. He lowkey made me enjoy math
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I had this professor my freshman year and would totally take another class with this professor
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very difficult to understand and learn from. Will say one thing then do something completely else on the homework or tests.

He lets us pick most quiz and test dates to best accommodate us and our level of understanding at that point. Math is one of those subjects where you need to do the work yourself and practice on your own time, so yes, while his lectures help to grasp new concepts, you need to put in many hours of your own time to get a good grade in this class.
He is a very kind, understanding person. However, he teaching style is unorganized. Don't expect to understand the topic with lectures alone. You have to somewhat teach yourself the material.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: One of the top two professors I had while at Ohio State and better than any I had at the Main Campus. Before class, he would go around the room and talk quietly and individually with each student to see how the student was doing. Very clear explanations of the subject (Calculus). A truly great teacher.

He is a good guy that is very intelligent. The one annoying thing was that some questions on the test would seem unrelated to what we learned and reviewed, or were worded oddly to be overly confusing. Once you get past the first test and into the business side of math, things clear up a lot and I started to enjoy the class much more.

Pandey is a great guy who will help anyone willing to try. The lower level classes are "harder" for him to teach because, in my opinion, its so simple for him. Try avoiding him for 130's but any higher math he is great

He is a bad teach that cant speak english very well and is confusing.  He is the worst math teacher I have ever had. I had him for 131 and refuse to advance because he is teaching 132. Avoid taking him if possible.
One of the best professors that has ever taught  math.
Excellent Professor!!!!

he sometimes gets confused, if you are confused on what he is saying, just stop him...he will correct himself.  i thought he was a great teacher other than that.  very helpful and willing to meet at anytime to help!

I am not very good at math and with Professor Pandey's patients I was able to get an A in his class.  I was full of questions every day and he didn't mind helping me and answering them.  Very nice teacher and he smiles all the time.  Really cares about his students.

he is the coolest math professor I ever had.  I am interested in learning math now, I was not before having him as my professor.  I love the way he treats students.  I will take more math and intersted in math education now.
Nice guy, works to make the class a little easier even though Math sucks...
Best math professor.. love him
No Comments

sometimes teaches wrong math material in class (example: taught 152 math in a 151 class); forgets to teach some chapters but still expects you to know how to do it--MAY go over HOW to do problem RIGHT BEFORE the exam
Okay prof, I just don't like math.
so incredibly easy. if you need the credit take this course. you can use chat gpt for almost every assignment.

Easy class. There are quite a few assignments, but they are not challenging at all. One thing I will say that it gets tedious because it's too easy. There are a couple of large writing assignments, but they are very do-able. Do them in advance!

Professor Sadler was a very good professor to have for an online class. He would post weekly updates about what was expected to be done for that week and I always found the material to be very engaging. I would definitely recommend him if this class interests you.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Excellent professor and class. Learned a lot of useful tools like iMovie and Garage Band that can help in numerous lines of work. Very lenient with deadlines, be sure to participate in discussions. Ben creates a welcoming environment where all ideas are considered. You will also receive peer review in front of the class for your work.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Fun lectures and gives great feedback on assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. McCorkle is a great English instructor. I've had his class previously, and feel that he is one of the best English faculty on the Marion Campus. He ensures that students get an opportunity to learn in the way that makes them comfortable, and offers opportunity to express creativity in his assignments. I would take his class again.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Ben is amazing. His classes are full of lively discussion, and they mix conventional literature with multimedia. He pushes the boundaries of what students expect when signing up for an "English" class, and that is what makes it so unique and enjoyable. You learn not only writing skills, and literary tools, but how they are effected by new mediums.
Believes in letting students discover and learn for themselves. Great class.
Ben is an Excellent Professor, He is truly interested in issues of the internet and digital media composition

Ben is a fantastic professor with an easy going attitude. He is genuinely interested in his work and his students; he enjoys a good lively discussion and promotes class participation. Very much enjoyed his class and look forward to other courses with him.

This class is already hard enough on its own but Doran definitely made this class even harder. He was not very good at making the material easier to understand that's for sure. If I didn't have a math tutor for this class I don't know how I would have passed.

reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational

reviews.comment: Difficult teaching style and requires a lot of out of class work to be proficient
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Get ready to read--Hilarious

reviews.comment: In my experience taking both ENGR 1281 and 1282 with Brooke Morin, she is very caring toward the student body and understanding with the workload of the class. She often encourages us to talk to her if we ever feel overwhelmed, and I have never felt uncomfortable or scared asking her a question. One of the best teachers I have had so far.
Not a very good lecturer.  Doesn't really take any interest in her students.  Would not recommend to anyone because there's better to choose from in the department.

Really glad I had Brooke for the robot design project. Makes it obvious that she wants to help everyone succeed. I didn't initially want to take the class/think I could handle the workload, but she's there to support every student every step of the way if you're willing to ask for help.

I saw the complaints on here and wanted to weigh in on behalf of the professor. Yes the class is demanding- Yes there is a lot expected of the student especially as a second semester senior. However, the information learned was the most relevant to the working world of any class I had at Fisher... Do not be afraid of a challenge.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He is so smart but he doesn't "dumb it down." He flys through powerpoints that aren't posted online. Difficult academic papers to read and present every week. Tough grading on exams that isn't always clear. The class is set up to be much harder than it needs to be. Book is recommended and is the only reason I know what's going on in the class.

He rushes through his powerpoints and doesn't post them online.  He has never returned a graded assignnment (homeworkexams) so you have no idea how he grades.  There are multiple hours of homework due each week.  Be prepared to read the book for this class or you will have no idea what is going on.  He doesn't care if his students do well.

Do not take his class !!! His class is like self-study. He skim through the slides, which are not posted and speak really fast. Even though you attend all the class, do well in the exam and homework assignments, you may not get a A level grade.

I took this class as an elective, but it was extremely hard for no reason. He is intelligent, but does not know how to teach the concepts so that students understand. A lot of reading and tough assignments. The tests are hard and all comprehensive. He doesn't help much when you ask questions either. He rushes through his slides which are not posted

The previous rating is a bit dramatic, but true in some aspects. I agree that with this being an elective class, I would prefer there to be a little less work. All tests are cumulative apparently, which no college student will enjoy. I also agree that he rushes through the notes pretty quickly. Overall, his class is tough for no reason

Really hard class. His lectures are ambiguous and he kind of skims through the material. He also  gives homework which is due on the Sat nights. Also, has a final group project which takes a lot of work plus a final exam. He is a little out of touch with what a normal class work load is like. Interesting class if you are willing to give up a lot.

He is a horrible professor. I have to teach myself most of the material, and his lectures are not clear. He spends most of his time during lectures just yapping and reading the slides. The group work isn't helpful to review, and the midterm 1 review is not useful whatsoever. Midterm 2 is kinda similar to the review, but not too much.

A lot of difficult homework. 10 attempts per each question. Practice exams are helpful as well as in class worksheets. It's recommended to go to class to work on the work sheet. Very helpful when you ask for help on the programming labs. Easy to lose focus during class.
go to class and you will be fine

Chris is a pretty good teacher. I took him for both of my engineering physics classes. His teaching philosophy with physics is also pretty cool, because he diverges from the typical physics book problems and gears his problems to 'real life' events. Also, if you take these classes, be prepared to do a little bit of extra work on programming labs.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Chrisman is a great math professor. His lectures clicked for me and are easy to understand. Tests were not bad. His lectures might be hard to understand however if you're not math-inclined. Currently in his MATH1172 course.

He's super sweet and funny as a person, but as a professor, I don't recommend taking his class. As someone who didn't take Calc in high school and then took his Calc1 class, he went through all the topics extremely fast. We would do a practice problem and then move on. Also, the way he taught the class was so diffcult. He didn't dumb anything down.

The questions on the exams are not like the exam reviews or the homeworks, or theres an added step to the equation thats supposed to be “known” to get the answer even if it hadn't been taught. you need to actually come to class to remotely get at what the lesson is about.

Often find it hard learning learning various topics as the instructor goes through math topics way too quickly without digging into the basics first. You must learn on your own to pass, even with that, the tests tend to be much more different than the actual review, tends to even differ from leactures. The test averages were about 60% and 59%

Tests are very stressful, always more difficult than the practice problems given. Class feels rushed, homework is more difficult than what you go over in class. However, he offers extra credit and partial credit on tests.

Great class and easy to learn! He does go quite fast in lectures so if you process things slowly it might be a little difficult. Easy to ask for help or clarification on in class questions or homework. Opportunities for extra credit. Tests are a little stressful. Highly recommend!

I really enjoyed having Micah as a teacher. Hes super nice and can really tell he cares about his students. He is eager to help if you dont understand something. Unlimited attempts on homework. My only critique is that the tests are hard, and each one is over everything that you learned, not just the last quarter. I would recommend this class.

Doesn't know how to lecture (the class is always asleep and she doesn't care), tests never cover the slides or flashcards she puts out, honestly a waste of my time. Only reason I'm doing well is because of the many writing assignments. Avoid this teacher unless you're bold and have no choices like I did

This is an online class, the homework and materials are easy to manage however Carol is the toughest grader and very non-specific when giving feedback. She takes the online portion way to seriously and is extremely nit-picky.

This teacher is awful. Avoid her at all costs! If you take one of her classes you won't get the grade that you deserve. During FranklinLive! classes she tries to trip up her students by asking silly questions. You have been warned.

Dr. Corkern is a wonderful professor and Introduction to Physical Anthropology is a great course. She covers the material thoroughly in class and does a good job of providing outlines, worksheets and handouts. This class isn't easy, but it isn't hard as long as you STUDY STUDY STUDY. It can be overwhelming with the amount of terms, but it's worthit
very laid back. always willing to help outside of class time if needed. overall great guy.

Has really great taste in music. At first I didn't like him but warmed up. If you ask for help, he will help you. There are a lot of notes to take in class so make sure you're there or buddy up. He does a great job of breaking down the material and helping you understand it. Learned so many interesting facts in his class, applies it to real world.

Ted is a great professor. I had the luxury of having him as both a high school teacher as well as a prof. and I feel he was exceptional in both capacities. He is very good at relating the material in a way that makes it easy to understand. Very funny
BEST AND EASY STATS TEACHER! DO take him!!

Very nice; seems a little gruff at first, but he genuinely wants to help students learn and has a sense of humor. Treat him with respect and you will do well. I am not a good math student by any means, but his class was easy and I learned some useful tasks.

Mr. Creasap gives you homework everyday so just be sure to do it all. After the first midterm, the book is useless cuz u use Excel a lot. This class is an easy A if u do all the homework and do fine on the exams. Goodluck :)
just go to class and you'll pass

Dr. Mosley has been my favorite professor on campus since I have been here. I took Anatomy 3300 and Anatomy 4300 with her and she was incredible in both classes. While the class was super fast paced, Dr. Mosley did her best to make sure we understood the material and were doing well enough. She was very kind and made class so much fun.

I liked the way Dr. Mosley organized this class. Lectures were super helpful, and the two labs a week really engrained the material into your brain. I think the tests were very fair- she does not try to trick you, so if you study the material, you will do well! Lecture for me was online and the labs were in person. Very doable class!

Dr. Mosley is an amazing teacher and cares a lot about how she reaches her students. Anatomy is already a somewhat difficult class to navigate but she does a very good job with being accessible for questions and being clear during lectures. She gives good feedback as well.

I don't know what this professor was thinking about. I took this professor's class, and we had 4 chapters to read on the first day of the semester, tons of lecture videos, and lab worksheets. we had our two quizzes every day, and each one took two hours to finish, and we had an exam in first week at school. rediculous professor
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. Mosley is extremely accessible outside of class and takes joy in answering any questions that students may have. She cares greatly about insuring that everyone is actually comprehending course material and provides numerous study guides. However, the class does move at a fast pace. Attendance and studying weekly is an absolute must to pass.
Class you definitely had to actually try lol but he was soooo cool super chill very funny 10/10 teacher

Material takes time to complete, but Dr. Daddis is phenomenal. Always helpful and compassionate, he is the shining star of the Marion campus. He is a genuinely kind professor concerned with teaching in manner that is understandable and relevant. 2220 can seem tough, but the math is simple. Daddis is a can't miss professor.
Good course.  Didn't want to take stats, but he makes it enjoyable.

Absolutely LOVE this professor. Always available during office hours and if you have a want to get a good grade and are willing to put in the time he will certainly work with you! Had an issue one semester where I missed over a month of class and he was more than willing to work with me to ensure I was able to pass the class. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!

He is a great professor and can tell he loves what he does and is more than willing to help you as much as you need. He makes it very hard to fail, if you come to class, ask questions, and do the work he will not let you fail! He is so helpful and is really there to help his students... Pretty much if you fail it is because you don't care! love him

I have this prof in 2 classes, he is not very approachable. In both classes, he runs out of class room as soon as it's over. He says see him after class but you can't, he is gone within a few min of it ending. Notes are confusing, not very organized. Wish he would give us full notes and just made us participate more. Seems like a good guy otherwise
great teacher
Gives you formulas but it is not easy.
You'd have to try really hard to NOT get an A in this class, haha. He makes it as easy as he possibly can, and you don't have to memorize any formulas. Very low-stress class.
Great prof.  Made statistics understandable!!
Great teacher! He doesn't make things easy for you but when you do have difficulties he is always more than willing to help until you get the concept.

READ THE BOOK! GO TO OFFICE HOURS! ASK QUESTIONS! He is a hard grader but go to class, pay attention, and study. He prepares students for future careers. He is a good guy and easy to talk to. If you need help go to him. He does help.

reviews.comment: Just go to office hours. I know he seems scary at first but he is the most genuine and caring professor I have ever had. Go ask the questions and read. Do your part for research and keep up with the material and you will be fine.

reviews.comment: Worst professor on this campus.

reviews.comment: Dr. Calede is the best professor I have ever had in my college career. He expects a lot out of his student but he will do anything it takes to ensure each and every one of his students are successful. He has prepared me for my career outside of college. I owe so much to this professor.

Easily one of the best professors I've ever had. Hilarious, caring, and genuinely cares about his students success. Classes are long but the dissections are fun. Beware, there is a group project that takes a great majority of the semester and is VERY research intensive. A LOT of memorization of class material.

One of the best professors I have ever had. He actually cares for his students and although the content is hard, he will go the extra mile for your learning. I had him for both anatomy & physiology. Don't be afraid to go to office hours regularly. If you participate and study the material given in class, then you will get an A.

One of the best professors on this campus. I would recommend him for anatomy and physiology. If you want a good grade, then study the work sheets. If you know the information on the worksheets, then you will get an A.
Everything is group work and learning on your own. Doesn't believe in powerpoints. Read the book to prepare for class. Doesn't lecture. Lots of diagrams and memorization. Hard homework
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Rivera was always very strict from the get go however is consistent and strict with everyone which is fair. I would take a class of his again since he has been reasonable and knows his stuff. There’s no textbook, just make sure to proofread and reread everything
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Rivera immediately set the tone that he had his ways and if you went any other way you would not pass. If you titled the subject line of an email wrong you got a three word reply titled "reading is fundamental" and nothing more. Prof. Rivera is a great guy and fun to learn from.His lectures however can be somewhat confusing.They are a bit all over the place at times.Entertaining class but I did have to read the book to make sence of some of the lectures.
fun guy, great music, good class

Really easy, yeah don't buy the book, don't bother taking notes either, if you just listen it's fine.  Only weirdness in regards to "the only good communist is a dead communist" deal
Easy A - requires very little out-of-class time.  Save $$$ and don't buy the books, you won't need them.  Lectures can be confusing, but if you pay attention, you will ace the exams.

He asked the class some pretty weird and uncomfortable questions for attendance that we had to answer them. He is a tough grader with no clear rubric for papers. reviews.comment:
If you actually want learn stuff Pete helps you a lot but if you care about your gpa don't take his class because he is a very tough grader

love this guy. always made class interesting and fun. challenging at times but he genuinely cares for his students and always provides feedback so you know where you need to improve. discussion is a big part of the class so participate and you'll be fine
Pete is a fantastic professor.
PETE IS A FINE SPECIMEN OF A MAN! WITH PETE YOU FEEL SMART, GET SMART AND YOU REALIZE YOU'RE DAMN SMART!

reviews.comment: Pete is a cool dude. He is really funny and kind of d!lfy ngl. The material we read and essays are interesting, and participation is key. I had him during peak pandemic and even then he was pretty personable. He doesn't put in grades until the end, though, which was a downside, but he does give feedback on every assignment. Overall a fun class!

It seems like he's wanting to raise up his score by commenting and rating his own teaching skills lol but this guy has called me out in front of the class multiple times due to his immatureness. He just wanted to make the class laugh by belittling me, which is fine, but picking on someone half your age is quite dismal. Good job!

Great professor, one of the best I have ever had. Super interesting conversations to be had in class and the material is somewhat interesting as well. Essays were common however Pete makes writing conventions easy to understand and provides lots of feedback.

If you pay attention and take his advice your writing will improve greatly. He's an awesome teacher is 100% willing to help you if you go to his office hours. He teaches different writing styles that I would have never known to use had I not taken his class, and I'm very happy I did. Try your hardest and your writing will improve.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: He definitely expects his students to be present and actively involved in class. He's a tough grader but he takes the time to give feedback on your papers, so definitely pay attention to that. If you genuinely want to be a better writer, he's a great professor to learn from. But if you're just looking to fill a GE credit, look elsewhere.
Loved taking Petes classes, definitely not an easy A but his clear outlines and ability to work with you make it much easier

Pete was a fun professor to have for freshmen year English but his class isn't easy. You have to work, pay attention, and participate if you want to do well in his class. He may come off as a bit rude sometimes but he helped me understand what the expectations for writing papers in college are.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Pete is quite honestly the best professor Ive ever had. He is kind and thoughtful and wants you to succeed. I took two of his courses and loved them both times. You will work for your grade, but its worth it. Take his feedback seriously and use it wisely. I promise you his course will teach you much more than skills in an English course.
Possibly the worst professor I have ever had. Zero social skills and rude in my opinion.

Pete is awesome. He takes time out of each class to talk with everyone and just kind of hang out for a bit. Hes a tough grader but he teaches you how to do better and really knows what hes talking about.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Hands-down the best person who has ever taught me writing (but you should have a good command of it before you enroll). He takes 10 min out of class to ask how you're doing each day and still gives much to learn and time to relax. You get about 3 papers and 2 short ones. I hate reading, but the literature was appealing, and he isn't a vocab-snob.

I was really expecting an honors English class to tear me to shreds, but Pete made this class so enjoyable and Im really glad I got to be apart of it. His use of witty anecdotes and use of light hearted profanity make him a real gem.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I have been writing at a college level since I was a freshman in high school and Pete is the first professor who actually TAUGHT me something new and useful. He destroys every simple-minded approach to writing that high school classes taught us and rebuilds something beautiful. Hands down the greatest professor I have ever had. 100/10 recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Overall, Pete will make you a better writer. However, he makes you work for it. You get up to three absences, so use them wisely. There is a quiz after every book that you read the day after it is due. (Cannot get through it with just spark notes.) Also one of the toughest graders I have came across, so try to use his feedback to your advantage.
One of the best professors I have ever had.

Pete is a great professor. You will do a lot of writing and he is a tough grader. But you will receive great feedback and improve your writing abilities. Pete is always willing to help point you in the right direction, answer questions, and is consistently available outside of class. His classes are not an easy A but you will learn a lot from Pete!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Ah Pete. I learned so much about writing in his class. I liked him so much that Im taking another one of his English classes. They are NOT an easy A. You must work for your grade and CANNOT wait until the day before to write the papers... this is coming from a professional procrastinator. Getting an A in Petes class was not easy, as most got Cs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I LOVED Pete, he was such a great guy. He made English a class to look forward to. He's super funny. The only thing I didn't like was that he was a super hard grader. A really good paper can maybe get you a high B. Most papers that are written overnight are most of the time C. You really have to put in the effort to make your paper stand out.

i don't understand his grading system at all, there are 5 pop quizzes over the books which i read and still could barley pass the quizzes. As well as 3-4 papers which are almost guaranteed C's. reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

Gives great feedback, really wants his students to do well and improve, but he needs to see you put in the work. Cares about his job and in return cares about really teaching students.

English is my second language! Peter was great teacher! He trys to make everything clear and he is there for help! He loves to give feedback for any questions you have! I enjoyed his class and till now I do not know my final grade! :))

Helps you and encourages you to find your own style of writing. He makes the class interesting. I learned a lot from him, his grammar voice is in my head when I write.

Pete has a huge chip on his shoulder. He can be very intimidating to talk to most of the time b/c he likes to prove that he is smarter than you. He likes to swear, eat in class and put his feet on the table. But will not tolerate sweats to be worn of any kind in class. He's passionate about his job and I did learn from him. I like his bow ties.
Love him! One of my favorite teachers. I'm going to miss him so much! Try your hardest in his class and you will be fine
If you like having your essays read and edited by the whole class then you`ll love this class.

He was the best teacher I have ever had! I hated english in high school and never understood it. But when I got to his class he made everything clear and fun. I never wanted to miss a class only because he made class worthwhile. He is awesome!
LOVED PETE!!!!!! amazing teacher, hilarious!!!!

Pete is a great teacher. Just because you have to show up to his class, have done the hw, and be ready to talk it about the material doesn't make him a bad teacher. He is an awesome teacher.... He connects with the students and treats us like adults! He is a great teacher!
Totally an intellectual badass.  The best professor going.
tough, but totally wonderful

reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

The grading approach lacks clarity and constructive feedback, leaving students uncertain about expectations even when they actively engage with the material and utilize external sources.

Fun class !
Loved the class! Fun and entertaining and I learned a lot

Went to office hours everyday. He helps more so go to his office hours!!!
One of my favorite professors! Class gets harder throughout the quarter, but as long as you attend class, pay attention, and ask questions you will get an A! He is very helpful..fun class

Dave's a great professor I was in both his 120 and 400 and the reason I'm still here is because of his belief in me that I can do I can make it. Dave love's his job,and his students. Excellent teacher.. As long as you attend class you will be fine..

All of the previous ratings and comments are spot-on for Dr. D. Schul. Great Prof. with a genuine desire to help you learn & achieve your BEST!
Good class, professor genuinely interested in teaching. Made the subject matter comprehensible.
it was fun

Class was boring and the tests were difficult.
He's a nice guy and kind of cute in a Seth from the OC nerdy way. However, he is NOT a very helpful teacher and you need to read the textbook thoroughly and study a lot to do well in this class.

David Steigerwald is an incredible professor. He is a master of the the content, clearly explains the field, picks excellent books for his class to read and discuss. He loves to teach students and help them excel. He wrote my grad school recommendation and gives excellent life and history advice!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Favorite Professor!! He is an amazing professor. He is so caring and will bring out the best in you.  He is very helpful with writing. You can turn in drafts before the final draft is due. The class is not easy but interesting. You need to go class to do well in it. Overall, he is a caring professor. I took this class on the Columbus campus.

Dr. Steigerwald is the best professor I've had at OSU. His lectures are engaging. Quizzes are easy since they are online. Papers are difficult, but he gives comments if you turn a rough draft in early.

He was so nice! Very helpful and funny! His class was very interesting and he made it very easy to pay attention. The only hard part was his writing prompts. He definitely grades like an English teacher, but I appreciated the challenge. His tests are all online and open book, so overall he's fairly easy.

I loved this class.Great techer very helpful.My only complaint is his online quizzes end on random days which makes them very east to miss.Other than that it was great he takes rough drafts for papers as well so easy if you put a little effort.
Be sure to read the books he assigns. He grades the papers like an English teacher, so it is difficult to B.S. a paper in his class. A great teacher anyways.

LOVED this class! Took it for my "easy" class but it was challenging, engaging. But the prof is an excellent lecturer, keeps things real and interesting. I liked going to lecture and even though we had to do intensive work on papers, the books were great to read and keep. Highly recommend this class if you're even a little interested in the 60s.

My favorite Professor so far!His papers are everything in the class.He is very willing to help if you have questions, or even if you want to email your paper to him before hand, he returns it with comments and fully graded.He then allows you to re-submit it for your actual grade. Great Prof. I highly recommend him! He's tough but   well worth it!=]

Professor Steigerwald is a good lecturer and will keep your attention. He cares about teaching and and is always glad to answer questions, and he will even read multiple drafts of papers to help with topical stuff and gramatical errors. Classes can be work intensive, and if you get an A you will have earned it especially in his 500 levels classes.

The class was probably the most difficult class I have ever taken. Very high curve, considering I did not pass an exam and still got a B+. I do not recommend taking as a first accounting class. You basically have to teach yourself everything because he does not do much. Very nice guy, just should stick to his physics degree and not accounting.
As a class it is like ripping off a band-aid, except for three long excruciatingly painful months.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dietrich is the best professor I have had in Fisher. If you work hard you will learn a ton and enjoy his class. Plus just get him to talk about San Diego if you don't feel like listening to accounting any more during class...

Professor Dietrich gives great and inspirational lectures. He really makes you think why we are doing Accounting this way instead of memorizing the rules. Most of the time, you find yourself understanding concepts just by listening to him talking about past experiences or random stories. This class helps me to decide to major in Accounting!

I enjoyed this class more than I thought I would. Got to do a presentation for a well-respected company and learned a bit about how public companies operate. Sometimes the class drags on, but it's not bad.
If you are considering enrolling in the Cardinal Health course, do not. Unless you are 100% set on working there, do not put yourself through this. period.

He is not a good professor. His slides just copy off of the textbook with less information. There are 3 exams with an optional final that drops your lowest exam grade. Most people take the final because he makes the exams super hard (average for the second was a 60%). I read the textbook and studied a lot to do well on the exams.

He is a horrible teacher. Every test had a horrible average (the final exam had an average of 66%) and it was very clearly a reflection of his teaching style. Tests did not correspond to assigned homework. Honestely as someone who gets good grades and truly showed up to every lecture, his class was an anomaly to me - so confusing for no reason.
He was terrible and made everything seem hard

Never prepared PowerPoint, just wrote irrelevant things on the board, and didn't learn anything, but he spent more time than econometrics, the return was minimal, his grades were mean, the worst teacher at Ohio State, none of them.
Grades unnecessarily hard for no reason. Not much work and 50 percent of grade is group project. Highly do not recommend taking any class with him.
He grades unnecessarily hard for a 2nd-semester writing class. Avoid him if you care about your GPA because even if you do everything right he will find a way to dock an absurd amount of points.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Honestly, his lectures were not horrid, but because the class is 500 kids, it is very hard to grasp the content. You must go home and review (I did not). Gave out extra credit in class via questions, which CAME IN HANDY for my final grade. If you do better on final than any of the other exams, it will replace their grade; it clutched. Good guy.
Just wow!

Not my favorite, however, he can be understanding. The course is pretty loaded, so do practice problems from Carmen and read your notes. Stata projects you're pretty much on your own, so get to know some classmates and talk it over with them. There was a curve at the end, however, he says that he doesn't usually do that.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you are willing to show up to class and put in the work you will do well enough to get a decent grade in the class. I'm sure many of the people who leave bad reviews don't show up to class. The material is challenging but he is always willing to answer questions and help students out. The class is hard so there is decent curve.

Professor Gibbons lectures are very long and monotone. There are two homework assignments for each chapter the first one is easy the second one is not. The Midterms are fair covering 3 chapters each, but the final is all 13 chapters and there is no guidance or review.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would not suggest others take Econometrics with Eric. He is not a good lecturer. 50% of the course grade is based on the 2 midterms, which are quite difficult and he does not prepare you well for. For reference, the median grade for the first midterm in my class was 52. So beware!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very smart, quality lecturer. Attendance was optional, but I didn't miss a single one because the material was heavy and he was good at utilizing the lecture time by teaching pertinent (on exam) material. Very exam heavy. Doing the homework and coming to his review sessions (the class before an exam) were what helped me to get an A.
Loved his lectures & how much he cares about his students.

Dr. Gibbons gets a bad rap, but is exemplary as a teacher. The material is tough for what it is, but he makes every effort to encourage learning as best he can. Always quick to respond outside of class, and will make time to help one on one. Make sure that you communicate if you have trouble, and keep on top of the readings. Truly a good professor.

I took him for Principles of micro and macro; micro was mostly in person & macro was online asynchronous. Cares about what he's teaching, but it's hard to tell. Lectures are pretty normal, sometimes boring (especially online). It's super useful to read the book chapter before class. With the small HW load, it's your responsibility to study to learn
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Eric was a solid professor and obviously likes his job but if you truly don't understand what he's saying in lectures, you're pretty screwed. He'll treat you like an idiot if you ask a question that he thinks is obvious. He does have some pretty nice advantages with class work and his exams though. Overall he is okay.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class was basically common sense with a few vocab words tossed in. Instructor was hands off, but I got a good grade with a few hours of studying a week, so it wasn't a big deal. Three tests, which have some weirdly worded multiple choice questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Eric's class was the worst, I can't imagine ever needing to take another of his econ classes. His lectures are drought and long, along with homework questions that don't come up in the quizzes. I wouldn't recommend taking his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took macro and micro with him. I understand that economics is probably a foreign concept to most people. Being good with abstract concepts like physics definitely helps, but you really must study. A lot of people get bad grades, but I really cannot think of a better professor, because his course will teach you in the best way possible.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Class layout is pretty simple, read a chapter in the textbook, take an online quiz on it, receive a lecture on that chapter, then do an in-class homework on that chapter in a group. If you read the chapter and show up to lectures you'll be fine. He can seem a little aggressive/mad if you can't explain how you got an answer or don't do your work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Gibbons makes you feel on edge to just sit in the classroom. His reads off of the slides and expects you to know and understand the material. Every question asked comes with another question, and if you give him something slightly wrong, you are given a an idiotic look. Garbage professor. Would have loved the material with a decent prof

Solid lectures, he writes everything that you need to put in your notes down on the board along with terms and definitions. He stays engaged with the class throughout to get an idea of how much everyone is comprehending. The only points are a weekly online homework assignment, two midterms, and the final. Studying on your own is a must though!
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Heard great things about Dr. Fermier in person, but if you have to take her online class I would avoid it. Good teacher, but leaves a serious backlog of assignments for certain parts of the semester so you may have 6+ assignments due in one week (usually during midterms or finals). Would avoid if possible
Great ASL 1101 instructor. The language is not an easy one to learn and there is a lot of content in the course. If you put the time and effort in, you will learn a lot about ASL.

She's a wonderful professor! If you are wanting to learn ASL, I highly recommend that you learn it from her as she is very knowledgeable and understanding. She often creates fun activities to get students involved and help to practice communication skills, homework isn't too difficult, and the exams aren't bad if you pay attention in class.
Best instructor I've ever had. Super nice, helpful. Really cares about the class. Must take for ASL!

I do like her a lot. She is super sweet and kind, but the syllabus is always all over the place and she can be sassy at times. Overall if you show interests in ASL and you work a good amount its a easy pass
An amazing professor 10/10, she's so sweet and knows her subject better than any professor I've ever.

This was my final course prior to graduating. This was the worst course I have ever taken at the Ohio State. My emails were never returned, lectures were posted late, and not one assignment was graded from February 2nd through April 14th. I would not recommend anyone take a course with this professor.

Dr. Mumper is great. The work load isn't heavy and cares about his students like no other professor I have ever had. He is willing to work with you and gives lots of opportunities to maintain an A. As long as you turn in homework, this should be an easy grade. His lectures are fun and exciting, exams aren't hard, and the book is interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Mumper taught a legitimately enjoyable class, attendance was for a grade up to a certain point but the lectures were fun to go to anyways, plus he never took the entire class period, and it was rare that he took up half of it. You will do well if you come to class and pay attention. Plus, he looks and sounds like Lin-Manuel Miranda.
Great teacher, very interesting lectures, very passionate about what he teaches.
Professor Mumper could take the most boring topics and turn them into the most entertaining and fun lectures.
I have never had a more understanding Professor, and Dr. Mumper absolutely cares about his students success in the subject. Absolutely recommend taking this course!
Not available during office hours lectures are boring and long.

Dr. Hamilton is a great person and is very knowledgeable about Chemistry. He teaches the information effectively, and held a weekly open session where if you had questions about the material, you were able to ask him. He also doesn't have a set grading scale, and awards grades based on his opinion of student effort and performance. Tough class tho.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. Hamilton is very smart and cares a lot about chemistry, however, his tests and quizzes were over stuff we never covered. It was super hard to keep up in his class. He posted practice exams but they were never over the same thing as the tests. If you attend OSU Lima; you are going to get stuck with him as your GE chem professor, so good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Hamilton knows his stuff, but you better know it too. Class average on tests was always low, but he never curved. Gave practice exams to study, but actual tests were more difficult. Hardest professor I've had yet. To sum it up, if you take his class, be prepared to work hard for your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Also an amazing musician, Domino Principle etc....

Makes sure you know everything about intro chemistry as thoroughly as possible. That inquires a lot of work but it leaves a perfect foundation for future classes. He is extremely simple with the material and is probably the most straightforward you can get. One of the best professors I've ever had.

An amazing professor, he really knows his stuff. Always keeps class interesting by adding quirky comments/stories. Always willing to answer any questions. Def. recommend this prof for your entire gen-chem series!

He was very well oriented and went into just the right amount of detail, not too much and not too little. You could ask questions at any time but most of the time the questions were answered in the presentation.
Very good teacher. Willing to help students. Conveys information well. Makes the class interesting. Easy work/willing to answer any questions.

At times I struggled to understand course material and had a difficult time, retaining information. However, Professor Elsaadany was always extremely invested in making sure that his students succeeded and that was very admirable. All in all, a very tough class but a good professor.
I completely think that Dr. Elsaadany was a great instructor. I think he taught the course perfectly.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Maybe the best instructor I have ever had. It is very clear the professor cares about the development of the students. Only good things to say about this professor.
Mr. Elsaadany was a pleasure to learn from. From keeping students involved to clearly laying out the material of each lecture, I would gladly take another one of his classes. Good show.
He is a very good instructor, he taught this subject very good and i can tell that he is interested in teaching and helping the students.

Dr. Elsaadany is by far my favorite instructor. He challenges me to do better and expects nothing but the best. To some students, his teaching seems harsh but the course is hard. If anything is unclear, he always quick to address it or respond right away If any student emails him. I look forward to having Dr. Elsaadany in my future courses.

Professor Elsaadany is greatly invested in making sure that every single one of his students are understanding the course material thoroughly and that every student does the best that they can which is greatly appreciated.

I love Dr. Mostafa, and he was probably my favorite professor this semester. I would highly recommend him to anyone taking this course. He does a great job at pacing and will reward you well if you do things on time!

Professor Elsaadany is an okay professor at best. He is mostly approachable after class for any questions but as for whether his answers actually help you on something important is a whole different animal. He's nice at most times, but can come off to be rather harsh in some of the meetings/feedback on small things. I probably wouldn't take again.
reviews.rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Dr. Emens was a wonderful professor. He pushed me to be a better student. He was willing to meet often outside of class to help me learn material. He is incredibly professional about the material, but very laid back in his approach to how you choose to learn. He was inspirational and helped me enjoy the material.
Horrible.  Jack was no organized and would be unprepared for class.   Would not take from him ever!!

Jack is a wonderful teacher. He is very kind and laid back. He made a class that wasn't all that interesting become fun. He is one of the kindest people I've met. There was a time I had a personal problem and missed the due date of the paper and he let me turn it in a week late. A lot of things were open note.
Brilliant man, probably the best teacher i have ever had. Incredible guy with an ability to teach students like no other.

How many instructors do you know who will have a study group at Denny's to help you? Only Jack so far! I was so nervous taking this class but Jack helped put me at ease. He breaks the material down so anyone can easily understand it. Even as a non traditional student, I learned so much in his class. Can't wait to take another one of his classes.

Jack opens every class with a joke. At first I thought this annoying but after I hear his joke, it helps me relax. I like his class a lot. He breaks down the material. He goes to length to help if you need it. I also like that I have an instructor who is involved with todays issues. His news articles makes for great reading, he's a great guy.
This class with a boring subject of constitutional law was made SO much better with Jack as the teacher, I want to take all of his classes.

Jack is HILARIOUS! He makes going to class fun, and we always had a good time in class. He started off every time with a joke of some sort. His material is easy to use and half of his tests are take home. I LOVE his classes!

I was very upset with Jack's class.He seems like a great person but makes the subject matter much more confusing then it has to be.I am good at math and got very high grades on everything but still ended up with a B.I have no idea how.If your a favorite you will do great =/
Jack keeps the class amusing. Doesn't really focus on one political viewpoint and a lot of fun assignments.

I LOVED him! he is always so happy and ready to teach class! he is so fun and makes fun debates! he knows what he is talking about and you can really tell he enjoys being there! i would love to attend another one of his classes!
I have had Jack for many classes and he is by far my favorite teacher. He is a great teacher and teaches in a way that is easy to learn. Jack is a great asset to Ohio State.

Other students like him because he is easy, but those that go on to higher level classes come quickly to realize how little they learned in Emens' class.  If you want an easy A, fine, but if you really want to learn something and be prepared for tougher classes in Columbus, you are getting ripped off.
Dr. Emens is a great teacher. He makes the class enjoyable and understandable. As long as you study and take notes, you should be able to pass the class! Really enjoyed poli sci 100!
What a great guy. I ill miss him. I wish he could teach all my classes. Just take notes, and you're good to go!
fun! fun! fun!
Favorite Prof ever!!!
great class. funniest prof you will ever have. take notes and study quizzes for exams.
very nice funny guy. easy A
Jack is the best!!! you'll love him... he would do anything for anyone.... and makes sure every student has the material... even meets you at bob evans before the exams to give you the anwsers!!!
Very Easy!
His classes are easy- even though he seems a little "scary" at first...he's a really great guy...he makes sure you know your stuff but he doesn't deliberately try to trick anyone...
Best teacher in the world.

jack is funny, which breaks the tension in the class room.  his class projects gets students involved and you have fun doing it.  he really knows his stuff and makes you think.  its an entry level course and he knows it, so he doesn't expect you to know ev
Soooo easy!  Any class you see he's teaching, take it!  Easy A!
Jack is a great guy! His classes are fun and you might learn a thing or two!
Very nice guy, hilarious. I suggest taking him for whatever classes you can.  Makes boring classes bearable.

Jack is very concerned with the well being of his students and is always there for them. He is interested in what he is doing, and loves what he is doing-- that is why he is so great. He is also a closet genius... but he would never admit to it.
EASY!!

I don't think that these classes are easy because most people have trouble with economics classes.  I think that Jack is a funny guy who makes class fun and since he is making it fun it is easier to understand the material.  I think that everyone who says it's easy is giving the wrong idea of the class it is that it is fun so it is easier to learn.
made it easy
This is the best teacher on the planet.  Everyone needs to take this class at OSUM whether you go to stanford or not.
Funny guy, easy class
Very good Teacher!! great jokes, enjoyable class!!!
HINT GET OLD TESTS!!  THEY ARE ALL THE SAME
No Comments
Jack is awesome!  Prolly one of the best people and profs you'll ever have in college.
No Comments
