Hilarious teacher! Won't sleep during this class!
She is a very sweet lady and will help you out with whatever you need! Just dont skip her class because it's for a grade. Work isnt as bad, just do it!
Wonderful teacher
thought class was very enjoyable would take it again loved her teaching method.
Great professor. class is really easy. come to class and do you work
Easy grader, mandatory attendance, and participation points. Just go to class and be engaged and an A is within reach
very good
Very nice teacher.
A great teacher with lots of helpful comments. Very enthusiastic about the class. A ##1 teacher
Definitely my favorite class of th quarter. Learned a lot, my teacher was helpful, and I even had a creative writing option! Totally awesome.

The exams are very straightforward all you need to do is literally look at the study guide. All assignments were easy and the final paper was kinda confusing but if you go to her office hours she great at setting you on the right track. she gives out a lot of extra credit if your class does bad on exams.

She mentioned in the beginning of class that students said the exams were hard but she didn't think so. Spoiler alert, it is difficult especially if you aren't a political science major. Most of the time the average was a 60% or 70%. Thankfully, she gave extra credit because she felt bad. Attendance is mandatory, and the class is lecture heavy.

Her lectures took up most of class time but they weren't boring at all. Homework was either an open book quiz or a discussion post. She makes a study guide for each exam. Exams were multiple choice questions and one written response question. The class was overall super easy I would definitely recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Franklin's 1100 class isn't that bad, it's easy to get a good grade if you put in the effort. Her Zoom meetings are once a week, go to it, participate in the weekly discussions and quizzes, study and you'll pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I was really excited about taking this class, but once I found out we were writing a Constitution for a group project, I wanted to die. Her exams are miserable and don't go over anything she teaches so be ready to read the ENTIRE textbook. I never will, and hopefully won't have to take her classes again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: The term paper is really the only difficult thing in this class especially if you do not know a lot about politics. Study guides for Exams are handed out and usually the same exact questions on the Exam. You can miss up to 3 classes without points being taken away.

Although lecture-heavy, class is very enjoyable. She doesn't expect everyone to blindly accept 'fact', but rather invites critical thought and opposing views. Always happy to have students interact, even with cynical or humorous (although relevant) comments. Excellent study guides for exams, very reasonable in every regard.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Lecture can get kind of long but they aren't bad. She can take get carried away easily but overall is a great professor. She is pretty lenient as long as you talk to her and let her know things.

Mrs. Sivakoff is absolutely amazing! She's super helpful to anyone who needs help in class, out of class, and even on exams. She makes lectures fun, provides us with great examples of ways to remember material and allows for wiggle room when students need extra credit, or extra help. Her class is easy and isn't one meant to make students stress.
Lots of material, fun labs

The most infuriating professor I've ever had. Bio 1114 She's unprepared, constantly confused, and makes students answer her questions. I studied nonstop using her slides and guides, and the exam was totally unrelated. When I asked for help, she brushed me off and blamed me. extremely condescending. Do not take her. DON'T TAKE HER.

She isn't organized during class and does not prepare before lecture. She often asks students questions but lacks experience with several lab procedures. We usually spend the entire lab trying to figure out what she wants from us. The tests don't match the study guide either you can spend hours studying, only for the exam to be completely opposite.

Dr. Sivakoff is great and kind! She offers 50+ extra credit points. Homework is easy if you use your notes, 4 quizzes are average difficulty, and the 5 exams you must study for as there is a lot of information you need to know (only top 4 counted), and the labs seem very detailed & excessive but as long as you do them, are easy points. Take her!

Scott is a wonderful professor and human being! He's generous when giving grades; literally, you don't have to do anything and you get an automatic 40-50% on the assignment. I learned a ton from the lectures, no need for a textbook. He does extremely well with students with mental health issues and will give extra time if needed. Fun class!

Scott is super nice and caring. He gives an average amount of assignments but is extremely generous when it comes to grading. I never used a textbook and was able to get an A just by listening to him lecture. Definitely take 2204 with Scott (whether it is for a GE or not)!!

Scott is a great professor, caring and responsive! LOTS of assignments which take awhile, but are given unlimited attempts and are easy. 2 exams that are also easy (open book, partners, internet). 200 extra credit points offered. It would be hard not to get above a 95% if you put in at least some effort. Take him!
One of the best professors I have ever had! He is caring and funny there will not be a single class of his that you wont be laughing in!

He is one of the best professors ever! Class isn't mandatory, but he like 14 pop quizzes that basically are "are you here, okay ten points" and "nope you are not here you still get 2 points". Then there are 7 "quizzes" and by that I mean they are take home and you can google the answers, though using the books are easier. Super easy A!!!
Excellent professor!

Scot ensures, through his 'blurbs,' that each student is heard and their needs are met. His class is easy, but he is very informative on the subject if you show up to learn. He does not grade harshly, and provides opportunity for extra credits throughout the course. He seems to genuinely care about his students. I would take his course again.
Scott is a very understanding teacher. Gives take home tests, and gives you answers you could not find. He is very helpful.

Easy professor. Gives a weekly (take home) quiz on Friday, Monday you go over it in class, and you turn in the following Friday. Lots of movies. Very accessible, gives good feedback. Have a couple of projects, but he is VERY flexible. I would recommend this class.
Loved Scott.  I retained his material and each class was engaging.  Wonderful class!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: AWESOME professor! Scott is so amazing and makes the subject really fun! u can work on like everything with a group. he's hilarious and just go to classes and listen to him talk and you'll get an A guaranteed
This teacher is extremly hilarious... and you'll learn alot....exams are very easy...if you want to boost your GPA, take this class

Scott is a very odd little man. An easy class I leave early everyday. He has no interest in what his class is doing, kids walk in late, talk over him, and leave in the middle of him talking, and he pretends like it's not happening.

I taken most all Scott classes and I always say taking his courses are a breath of fresh air especially if your struggling in other courses. He is the easist, sweetist, professor I know. If you need a break from alot of work, a certain class, get your GPA up or even just to maintain good grades, Scott Fisher is where you want to be.
Really awesome professor! He's one of the strangest, yet most fun and friendly professors I've ever had. All you really need to get an A is to try your best. I highly recommend his classes!!!
he is very helpful and funny teacher. i enjoyed his class.he is also freindly guy

He's a good teacher and this class is easy.  He goes off on tangents ALOT and usually half the lecture wasn't even about the class which got annoying, but its an easy A.  I left halfway through almost everyday and still got an A.

Scott is amazing. So far after 6 quarters, he is the most memorable. He makes the class interesting and the discussions are very open to anything, but somehow remains related to the subject. The class load is next to none. Class never felt like a burden to attend. Put effort into the exams (that you design) and you'll have an A and a great time
Awesome. I love Scott.
MY FAV PROFESSOR EVER!!!!
He tends to ramble in his lectures, but he is overall very knowledgable and makes his classes really fun. Midterms are un-orthodox, but easy. Love this class!
Oh my gosh ... he is my all time favorite teacher! I took one of his classes and next quarter I am taking two more. I can't get enough of this guy!
Awesome he is so funny and smart!
oh man i love this guy, take this class if your not an idoit!! w00t Scott ftw !
Funny guy, He has a lot of interesting stories. puts mythology in a way thats in present lingo.
No Comments

If you can follow his stream of conciousness, you'll love Scott's classes. He's an unconventional professor in the best way possible. Where else are you going to learn about ancient Egyptian diseases and lsd trips in the same sentence? :P
Very easy and a nice guy.  Talks about random things and the class is boring at some times, but the websites he goes to and stories he tells are awesome.
I would recommend this class to all first years!

Very good professor. Textbook was a waste of $$$; I didn't even read it after the first 2 weeks.  The class wrote most of the questions for the exams.  Relatively easy to get an A.  Some lectures were confusing-he likes to go off on tangents.
Very great teacher, very easy to get an A. Make sure to read the book, and participate. The final is usually just a small presentation. Very approachable.
This guy r teh pwn hxc.  (Translation: "Cheerio, old chap, he is a most skilled professor!")
very nice and easy.

Amy is a great professor. She is enthusiastic about her lectures and makes it her utmost priority to make sure her students understand the material. She is very approachable outside of class, and is fun to talk to. I only wish she could teach every subject, so I could enroll in all of them!
Everything is in writing. If you miss a detail she takes points. I feel like she takes more points away if you don't attend class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Ethics in Engineering is pretty easy with her, participation is big and lost after missing 3 times, all Exams are verbatim from the review packets, the essays are short, but the grading is quite easy. Great Lady, pulls students aside to ask how they are doing pretty frequently.
Her exams are only 13 points and each question is a point so its really hard to get a good grade. You have to have everything exactly correct

Dr. Friesen was an incredibly enjoyable professor and very helpful at every turn to help anyone who needed it. She was so helpful for me that it has genuinely impacted what I want to do later in life. I would definitely recommend Dr. Friesen for anyone interested in philosophy.

Mrs. Friesen is a very nice professor,  and although her lectures are very fast paced and you feel rushed she is a caring teacher.  This class would be perfect if the topics were covered broader and more in depth.

She was so helpful!! Before almost every exam I went to her office hours and discussed the material with me. She was genuinely interested in how I was doing in her class. She even valued my input about the material. But her class was not easy. I definitely had to study A LOT and the writing prompts are not high school stuff.

I did not enjoy this class. Friesen has very long powerpoints and i had a hard time understanding what was going on. You dont really need to buy the book, we have chapters to read but theyre hardly ever mentioned the next day. The best thing is that she has study guides for the exams, so its easier to get an A even when you dont pay attention

Great class.  I personally enjoy knowledge of how human beings behave and why they do what they do.  This class is pretty cool, sometimes it can get confusing and there is a lot of reading... but she will break it down for you until it DOES makes sense.  Overall i'm enjoying my time spent in her class this semester.  PHIL- Ethics 1300

I loved this class! She is my favorite professor yet. She is extremely clear and helpful. The material alone is difficult to understand but she makes it very comprehensible. Attendance is vital but the class is interesting and not painful to sit through.

Honestly loved the class but MUCH harder than it needed it to be. Essay exams, tons of readings, 2 major papers, and WEEKLY writings on prompts she gives you. Too much work when you have other classes to study for also.

This course was interesting and the professor taught it well. She obviously cares for the subject and portrays that to her students. You must attend class to take notes, which are used openly on quizzes and exams. Pay attention.

If you want to pass, class attendance is mandatory. The examples she uses and the majority of test questions come directly from class examples and info. 1 quiz and both exams were open book, but that doesn't mean they were an easy A. In fact, I made B's. It's an interesting class--go, get into the discussions. Plus, she bakes cookies on test days.

Until I took Calc 2 with Dr. Friesen I never realized how much work my math really needed. Chris likes to make you use a lot of algebra on exams and quizzes along side with the calc part so if you're used to using a calculator on everything like me start going back to the basics. He gives a boatload of homework that mostly is what is on the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Chris wants every student to know math REALLY well. If you take his class, he will assign 'optional' book work. This book work is NOT optional as it is the basis for almost all exams and quizzes. If you don't do it you will definitely feel it. He also goes around the room and asks people questions to keep them attentive in class, so pay attention!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I had him for Trig, Calc 1, and Linear Algebra. Best teacher I've ever had! His lectures are very clear and concise. Very caring. Go to his office hours if you need help. TRUST ME! Test questions are directly from the homework. NO CURVE.  Just make sure you know how to do all the homework questions and you'll be fine. I'd take him again if I could.
Class material was easy in the lecture but the quizzes and test were harder questions based on info from lecture. Very smart but doesn't do a good job explaining hard topics.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Gives you all information you need to do the homework and pass tests in class. There is a textbook but you can pass without it. Gives one example of each kind of problem. Will answer questions but condescending. drop 2 lowest hw and quizzes. add part. and attdn. WAY TOO MUCH HW one due every class. Final exam exceptionally hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Really nice and really funny guy. Is maybe the smartest professor in school. But class is a ton of work and youll need to know how to do 100+ problems for a test. I didn't have trouble performing well in the class but it still was a lot of work for me and I'm a math major. Half the class dropped after the second exam
Class is very laid out and he will teach you everything you need to know in order to succeed, great guy and demonstrated a tremendous amount of knowledge in the subject, take his class!

Very good teacher, very friendly and helpful. However, this class is A LOT of work, and doing all of it doesn't guarantee a good grade. He takes off points if you do the problem different from he did. He has a lenient scale but this is necessary for the way he grades. Very smart, but expects you to be as smart as him.
Can't answer student questions, and can only teach word for word from slides.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Susan is the most monotone person you'll ever meet and gets confused very easily, sometimes we wondered if she even knew her own slides. She is a very nice individual and means no harm, just not the greatest professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She reads of the slides word for word and makes backhanded comments about student engagement. She can't answer questions well, she goes too quickly, and doesn't even actually teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Avoid at all cost.

Almost all of the assignments are group projects completed during class. Before class is over, she goes over the answers and allows everyone to change their answers so it's difficult to not get 100%. The lectures are prerecorded and very difficult to follow as the course material is pretty heavy. However, she's great at answering questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Her class was not very good to say the least. She would tell you how to fix something like a lab occasionally, you fix it as she wants and would give you a lower grade than the original. Grades took forever and most of the grades would change many times, her tests weren't well made. If you had issues with anything she usually wouldn't be helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She does give some sort of quiz everyday, but it isn't that hard and it is only for participation points because she expects you to come to class. She does read off power points, but what prof. doesn't? The lectures can be tedious, but it's biology. There really isn't ample ways to make it fun. Dr. Gershman is excellent and ALWAYS willing to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: My evol prof, she read off the slides the entire lectures and gave hard pop quizzes each lecture. You know what makes a great professor? One who can explain the material in different ways for their students to understand. If you had a question for her regarding what you didnt understand, she pulled out her laptop and read the lecture back to you.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I have had Gershman three times now, for two bio classes and evolution. She gives pop quizzes but they are only worth a few points and arent difficult, you can work with other people on it, they are just a check for you, not a penalty. She is willing to answer your questions and clearly enjoys her subject. Follow with the ppt she posts and easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Caring

reviews.comment: Reads word for word off slides, gives a pop quiz for every single class that is actually pretty tough so if you have kids or youre sick you have limited options, boring, not engaging, I nearly fell asleep for each class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Gershman's bio was the worst bio I have ever taken. Every single one of her lectures were verbatim from the powerpoint, and going to class to learn material is useless since she posts the ppts online. All you have to do is memorize those slides and you will pass with flying colors. Only reason to go to class is for those daily 3 pt quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I failed bio with another professor who was ridiculously hard so comparatively she was great! She has fill in the blank notes, it helps follow along. There are a lot of 3pt quizzes so its best to be in class. The reading review quizzes are long but helpful for exams. I like the set up of her exams: T/F, mult choice, some diagrams, etc.

Terrible professor. Avoid her at all costs. If you ask a question that can't be answered or fixed there in class, then it won't be answered. There about half of the slides that were missing material on one chapter and multiple people told her, but she never fixed it. Lectures are just read off of the slides, no other material or words are given.

She's a nice enough person, but honestly, she's the worst professor I've ever had. It's not that she doesn't care or want you to pass, she just presents the material in an awful and confusing manner. I got nothing from the lectures at all, but she gives so many quizzes you have to go. She has online quizzes for each chapter, but they don't help@all

Gershman was my favorite professor this semester. You can tell she's really into her job. She's extremely helpful, easy to approach with questions, and won't make you feel like an idiot. You'd better attend class though, because there are frequent pop quizzes. The lab is mandatory.

Professor Gershman is great. She really wants to help you learn the information. She knows it's hard and she does all she can to help you through it. She gives tests on Carmen that you can take many times and prepares you well for tests. Beware, she gives pop quizes pretty frequently. She has you do a lot of group work so sit by soneone you like.

If you accidentally mark something wrong, you cant go back and edit it. If your unable to complete an assignment for any reason your unable to go back and submit it. The course isn't difficult, but its Incredibly tedious because theres no room for correcting mistakes. its so much harder to focus when you have to constantly recheck everything.

She teaches both an online and in person version of this class, and I wish that I would have just taken the class online. She barely ever responds to her emails, and she won't let you redo assignments if the instructions are unclear or you have issues with technology. You won't get an answer if you ask her about tech. "Ask a friend to help."
She never checks her emails and doesn't care about the students at all. Never admits that she is wrong. So boring to listen to. I would rather watch paint dry than sit through her class.

I actually took Professor Genova for History 3017, but that wasn't an option. Homework consists of one discussion board per week, and he provides exam study sheets for both the midterm and final.  What is on the study sheet is EXACTLY what is on the exam. Easy A if you're willing to put in the work and study the exam handouts.
would take again, lots of reading
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: na na na na na
heavy writing however Professor Genova is an amazing teacher and gives great feedback/help. His lectures are detailed and he does a good job of explaining things.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: Boring Teacher, Didnt listen to many if any lectures, Gives study guide of exactly whats on the test. Easy A from Study Guide
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Genovas class setup is one of the best I have been apart of. Wether you come and pay attention or not is really up to you. There is a discussion on Carmen each week, followed by a couple papers, a mid-term and a final. Study guide he gives is exactly like the test. Minimal textbook use, mostly all info needed is in lecture. One of my favorites
You need 6 books for this course.
Extremely inteligent Professor with wonderful lectures. You may have to work hard in his class but understanding the material is well worth the effort!

Although the class format is easily laid out, the subject matter for this course was difficult. Also, would have liked to have more class discussion versus dry lectures. Dr. Genova is friendly and respectful but he is very serious overall. Good for the serious history majors most likely.
No Comments
No Comments
He's the goat just pay attention not hard

THE GOAT! He is an AMAZING professor! He is so nice & he dumbs it down to the BASICS! He makes it easy to understand & cares about his students. He gives free points if you show up to class. There are 3 exams & 8 quizzes, but he drops 1 exam & 2 quizzes. With the remaining 6 quizzes, 3 are free points. I recommend his class as you won't regret it!

He does a good job explaining different topics. He said we needed a textbook for the class but not needed. Definitely need to show up to class to get credit. Sometimes he would give us free points on quiz's or homework's just for showing up. Utilize the homework's for exams and quiz's. Easy A if you show up and pay attention.
great professor

BUSOBA 2321 with PE's & MBE's was organized well. We used lot of excel. Had to watch videos prior & the lecture became little repetitive. Homework & solutions were posted on the Canvas. Prof Garapati provided many resources to succeed. I could have used these resources on Canvas though. Prof Garapati is a very nice person and excellent teacher.

I took his BUSOBA 2320 course at the OSU Marion. Professor Garapati explained the material very well. He actually wanted us to learn unlike most teachers. Overall the class was well organized and challenging in a good sense.

Took his BUSOBA 2321 course online through the Newark campus. Through the semester we were unable to see our course grade and had few examples for our excel assignments. We also did not receive very good feedback on most assignments especially near the end of the semester. Overall, the resources we had in class were not very good.

Vijay is the best when it comes to calculus. His lectures consist of tons of example problems that are nearly identical to the problems on homework assignments and exams. The class can feel difficult and fast paced at points but he is always willing to help when asked. 6 quizzes, 4 exams, somewhat difficult but he gives lots of extra credit.

Honestly, he was one of the best professors I've had throughout my entire college journey. I wish I could take his class again. He is extremely lenient when grading and makes sure everybody passes his class as long as you put in some effort. He is a very good and caring man.

Amazing teacher! Love the way he lectures with lots of examples and encourages everyone to ask questions. Accessible outside of class, and does his very best to make things easier on students, acknowledging their workloads. Dropped lowest exam, 3 quizzes, and 5 HWs as well as giving 3 A+ quiz grades just for attending class; exams are 110pts./100).

One of the best math professor I have ever had. He gives plenty of examples and explanations in class. The professor was always open to help students out and encouraged them to come ask him questions to get help. Learned a lot from this class!

His lectures are very dry. This is mainly because he doesn't engage with the class often as he moves at a very brisk pace. The class is graded on daily homework, six quizzes, and four exams. Falling behind, especially in the beginning weeks can quickly make this class impossible to pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The best math teacher you can have at OSU Marion, follow his directions, ask for help, do the homework and you will pass!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Wish I could have him for every math class! Listen to his advice and you will easily pass

Hes a really cool guy. If you have a question, he will help you until you understand it. You really dont need the book because he goes over a lot of materials in the notes. As long as you pay attention, take notes, and actually try, you will pass with a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This dudes a G. Super nice, to understand the coursework you should show up to class, but its possible to catch up. The quizzes he gives out are straightforward, and he gives out a lot of free points. He makes a boring class not miserable.

MUST show up to all classes, his lectures are easy to follow and he will do homework questions in class if you ask, but if you skip classes you will feel lost. Easy to do well on his exams, he will take questions right from the textbook to put on the exam. Will give you partial points for missed questions if you do the steps right.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Mary Guyton was incredibly nice and obviously cared about helping her students succeed. It was a very easy class but important and enjoyable nonetheless

Professor Guyton is so nice & truly cares about students! She takes the time to give feedback on assignments & grades things very fairly. Took her class (intro to health and wellness) during Covid-19 so it was all online. She set up modules that had all assignments (about 10-15) due by a certain date so you could work on them at your own pace.
This First Aid and CPR class was one of my favorite classes! The professor is great and has many stories to share! She also brings snacks!

Reads straight off the slides, doesn't help during office hours. Extremely tough grader and will expect you to understand the material that is brand new fully. Don't take this course at Marion take it somewhere else.
A ton of homework each week. Lectures are super boring and it seems like she doesn't even know what she is teaching.

Given the difficulty of this class, I figured it would be helpful to get help from her office hours and maybe some TA help, but her hours were extremely limited. She is a tough grader on the projects and exams. She has a hard time connecting with the class and I see why. I would avoid taking Software 1 here and take in Columbus.

Currently taking her software 2 class, she goes above and beyond to explain concepts in a way anyone could understand. Email responses are quick and well written, you can tell this professor really cares about the success of the students, and making sure they are well prepared for future classes. Makes great use of visuals to explain concepts.10/10

I had her for 2321 and 2231, her Foundations 1 exams were the easiest exams I've taken in CS, her Software 2 exams were the usual difficulty. Her lectures are super helpful though because she goes over really tough practice problems and makes sure everyone gets it, she also responds to emails with good responses. Overall a good prof.

Good class, good professor. She helps out a lot with in labs and pre labs if you are stuck or need help. Provides study guides and practice sets before quizzes and exams which help a lot. As long as you don't miss a ton of class this class is pretty standard

Luckily, the material came easy to me. If I wouldn't have picked up on the class then I would've struggled. She is sometimes hard to understand, but makes that up in willingness to help out. Even during labs she isn't hesitant to help out if you're stuck. The one thing that I noticed throughout her class is that some directions can be unclear. Ask!

The class is very interesting but she has a hard time explaining things. If this is your first coding class then you are not in luck. It is very hard for beginners, she is always available to talk to students and help them. It is important to listen even though the lectures are very boring. The homework is very difficult too and it most definitely

Dr. Mirzaei is a fair professor. She seems very knowledgeable but not always able to express the information well. The lectures cover the same information as the textbook activities, but she gives extra credit for perfect attendance. Lectures mainly serve as a time to ask questions about homework. Quizzes are easier than the homework problems.

I took this class as a prerequisite for my major program and let me tell you... this class is extremely hard. Unfortunately we didn't get a curve on any of our assignments and the exams were very difficult. Professor Mirazei is a very nice and caring professor and she takes time out of her day lot of the time to help students. Great professor tho

Flies through lessons and gives a ton of homework and quizzes. If you don't have a lot of prior knowledge you're going to struggle. He barely teaches at all. He also doesn't allow phones so you can't use them for help.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Course is not difficult if you watch his short online lectures before the in-class lecture. If you have a great understanding of Algebra 2 or even Pre-Calculus, this course is easy. Participating in class and asking questions is also key to doing well.

Hobbs makes sure you know the material and are not lost in the class. Class is structured by a short lecture and then a worksheet that is due by the end of class. Really easy class as long as you study and have a decent understanding of Algebra 2.
Good professor helps well with questions and really explains them gets mad easily also tough grader but overall good teacher

He rushes his lectures and doesn't give enough time to work on homework. He is without a doubt the worst math professor I've ever had. If you have to take math 1148 please do not take this professor!!

This Professor was unprofessional throughout the course. He only lectures for 10 minutes, accused students of cheating on their placement tests due to low test scores, gets angry about students taking notes, acts like you're wasting time if you ask questions, won't leave room during SEIs, doesn't give partial credit, and is overall unpleasant.

He is great professor, I took two courses by him. His method is short lectures in the beginning of class and then you get a work sheet that breaks down and outlines each topic. These are great took look back on when reviewing for tests. However, he can be a tough grader with tests and homework. don't rush worksheets, he always helps with questions
I really like professor hobbs. Great teacher

-Has favorites
-Lectures are very rushed
-If you ask for help it feels like he doesn't have time 
-Gets mad if you take notes in class
-Not enough time to do in class assignments

Took him twice and although he has a good teaching method (only using small amount of time for lecture and using rest of time to help students with worksheets) lectures were very rushed and worksheets/homework was always 10x harder than the worksheet. Hard to contact outside of class and when asking questions never gives a straight answer
Takes off lots of points for small things and doesn't understand the material he's teaching. Makes trying to get a good grade difficult.

- Attendance freak 
- To much homework
- No after class help
- Tough grader
- Seating arrangement in college
- Tedious about small things
- Not great with lectures 
- As a professor, not worth taking class with him
- No extra credit at all
The tests were tough but if you have the online book, theyre easier. Luckily, he scaled grades and the papers were somewhat interesting, too.
The class was very clearly laid out and I appreciated his use of Carmen.  He is accessible and will work with you if there is a need.
Very easy class. Open book midterm and final. Easy A.
He is very relatable and really wants to do everything in his power to help everyone succeed.  You just have to be willing to put in the work.
Leads good discussions.

He has a lot of experience through OSUM, 30 years he told us, but I'm not really sure how in that 30 years he never learned how to communicate with students. I've taken two of his classes, and to the day I am still scared to ask him for help or seek him out during office hours. He is very rude, and unkind . DO NOT take him unless necessary!!!!

This class was an awful experience. Soft spoken, extremely difficult to understand, & very condescending .Takes points off for what ever he feels like. He makes you do a lot of homework and only grades five random problems. Takes questions straight off of the review guide which was very helpful but overall never want to take his class ever again.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Great class.  He doesn't really teach "methods" for teaching the subject matter.  It is a teaching math class.  But, his actual style has a methodical "baby steps" quality so it's a great refresher and a good example of how to teach the material.  He's quirky but it helps keep you engaged.  He's on a mission and moves fast so keep up.  Easy class.

Really clear and wonderful professor. Says "Am I Right" all the time, which is fairly amusing. Easy to take notes to. Collects 5 random homework assgns. a quarter. Amazingly nice, and extremely helpful if you need it. Only qualm is that his tests are timed to 60 mins. Almost everyone finishes easily in that time.
Moves a little quick in class, but explains everything well. Any question just ask before, durring or after class. A little hard to understand at times, but you can pick up most of what he is saying
Terrible instructor.  Never answered questions fully that the students could comprehend.  Hard to understand at times.Stay away from his class if you can.  Okay?
Is a great teacher, moves kinda fast, but still he is very explanitive and allows notes on quizes, highly recommend this teacher.
Russ's class is TONS of fun, and he's a good teacher ~ I would definitely recomend him to anyone.
I had him three quarter in a row and although at times he was hard to understand if you needed help or something clerified he would not hesistate to do so.  He was also a very understand.
VERY HARD TO UNDERSTAND. allows notecard with formulas though (only upside to his class).
Very thorough let us out of lectures early most days not good at explaining concepts, just definitions
Overall a good lecturer, He stays after class to help anyone and is a nice guy.

I took the 8 am classes and it was tough, I think I only showed up for the first two weeks but it does not matter at all, but make sure you go to the Recitation, I feel like his TAs are the ones who do real work. Anyway, great guy, extremely detailed, pretty slow speed and his slides are all home-made which is so touching.

Overall good teacher. He really knows what he is talking about. Sometimes his lecture can drag on but if you go to his office hours he will clear up any confusion you have. He really wants to help students and as long as you reach out to him when you don't understand.

His lectures are boring, he just goes over the textbook. If you're having trouble with a concept, attending lectures can help because he explains things in great detail, and is somewhat repetitive. That said, you don't need to go to get an A. Just do the suggested problems and show up for recitations. His TAs grade literally everything.

Has you turn in and attendence pass at the end of each class worth 1 point, so make sure you do not miss a lot of class.  If you pay close attention you should be fine.  Seems to get a bit irritated when questions are asked while he's teaching due to the fact it takes away class time.  I'd recommend asking any question you have before/after class.

Great professor. He has control of his classroom and commands respect for himself as well as fellow students. This is not the professor to take if you are a slacker or like to text message during class; however, if you want to truly learn, pay attention in class, and commit yourself to studying, you will do very well in Dr. Aly's economics classes.

This dude sucks!  Poor classroom prep...missed 1/4 of classes to do his real job...short temper...no time to do in class assignments...ridiculous take home final...50 multiple choice, 10 T F,and another 6 pages of charts and diagrams.   Only half was taught in class  Very stuck on himself!
I would recommend NEVER taking one of his classes

This class is difficult. The power points are not detailed at all. It's just a list of what he covers in class so it is no help-doesn't follow book. He snaps when people ask him questions or don't understand. Goes very fast so it's hard to take notes. Expect a lot of group work and a presentation at the end. Too bad, he seems to be very smart!

You have to turn in attendance sheets to earn points in his class. He is very knowledgeable no doubt, but needs to work on his people skills. His power points aren't that detailed but he also doesn't follow the book that he said we HAVE to buy. Makes the class hard. There's lots of group work so sit by people you like to work with that won't slack.

Overall, good professor. Very nice how he puts everything on Carmen. I got through the class with an A and didn't have a textbook, just printed out and studied his powerpoints. If you can't figure out homework, search online; most of his course material is from a set. The take home final was great.
Excellent teacher--doesn't tolerate slacking, but will go to great lengths to help you if you're trying hard.
Very easy class. Teaches right from the book. Short temper, amusing to watch when angry
excellent class
second midterm-exactly the same as the practice.  Final-easy take-home

One of my favorite professors. Very friendly and easy going. The class isn't, too hard but does take some studding to get a good grade. Everything is on Carmen. Very helpful if you need it. He keeps it interesting and rarely keeps you the whole class period. I would defiantly recommend Econ with Dr. Aly.
wonderful, great, can not ask for better instructor in this class.  knows his stuff and real friendly and helpful.
Great professor, would recommend him for econ classes. Very helpfull and demonstrates his knowlege and interest in economics in every class.

Teaches very easy classes that require a minimum of effort to excel in. You are required to learn some info, but it is nothing too strenous. It can be picked up just by attending class and reading your book.
Absolutly horrible prof. Do not take any of his classes. He brags that the average grade in his classes are a C-
wonderful professor, very intelligent, knows his stuff, you work for the grade and you get what you deserve, but overall very enjoyable
No Comments

People are rough on Ibrahim because he teaches hard classes. I like him a lot. He is clear cut when it comes to what you need to know for the tests. He also is extremely dedicated to helping you, if you put in the time. He holds review sessions that are really helpful! Definitely go them, pay attention, and you should be fine.
Wow this was an awful class
Can't understand him and can't read his handwriting... Prepare to teach yourself
Useless professor. Very nice guy but worst professor at osu hands down and thats saying something there. you will more than likely fail if you take this class

Teacher is hard to understand sometimes due to accent (sit in front of class). Teacher can be unclear, and have different methods of computation than lecturer. If you took Calc in HS, you should be OK. Otherwise, good luck. Teacher does know his math however, and is a sincere, nice guy who wants to help. It's just difficult to understand at times.

This teacher is awful. First day of lecture he literally sky rockets right into something that no one even has a clue about, doesn't even take the time to explain how the class is gonna go, no syllabus. Really strong french accent, cant make out what hes saying. DO NOT take this class unless you just wanna fail it and take it again...

Extremely picky for no reason and expects you to put way too much time into a class that I only took because I needed a GE. Assignments are doable but getting an A is more trouble than its even worth.

She is an amazing, caring lady! She is constantly in contact with her students to make sure we are all on the same page. The material itself is difficult and overwhelming but she tries her best to make it understandable for us. She genuinely cares about her students and she's been my favorite professor so far.

This is one of the best classes I have ever taken! If you get the chance to take this Professor, do it!! Everything is beautifully laid out and very easy to follow. If you arent a PowerPoint note taker and a self learner, this class isnt for you. Overall, all the exams were fair and you get 2 attempts for every quiz. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Had to constantly be checking online because due dates would sneak up on me. Missed a point or two on almost every assignment for the tiniest details that I would have to read her mind to know about. Knew several people who dropped the class. If you do all the assignments you'll pass, but an A is more trouble than its worth.
Best teacher I've ever had. She is absolutely amazing, and I would recommend her to anyone!

Online class that required face to face meetings for exams only. Instructor was kinda picky about the way she wanted things done, but swhe really did know her stuff because she is actually a nutritionist for the OSU sports department that teaches occassionaly, not a full- time prof that thinks she knows everything. Kind of work intensive though.

I LOVE TURNER. I have had him for both gen chem 1 & 2 he is a wonderful professor and a very genuine guy he wants you to do good in his class! He's been teaching chem and knows how to make it make sense. I recommend to get an A in his class to read the lecture before class, practice problems, and office hours

Probably one of the greatest chemistry teachers you can get. The lectures are exactly what's on the exam, and he works with you so you understand the concept. He has office hours, and email as well if you need extra help. His tests aren't hard especially if you do the practice he gives. He also gives extra credit and drops the worst post & pre lab.

Amazing teacher, cares about students, easy grader and is super nice. Offered lots of extra credit points to the whole class. Made chemistry enjoyable.
10/10 would recommend to take.

I took Chem with him and he clearly made it the easiest he possibly could. He cares about his students, and during labs when we would have problems with the online server, he always changed the grades and graded lightly on exams. If you have to take Chem, take it with him!!
Dr. Turner was a great professor. He's fantastic at explaining hard chemistry concepts and conveying them into things that relate to everyday life. Don't be afraid to ask questions!
I took Chem 1250, Chemistry for Engineers. It was a fast-paced class, but Dr. Turner made everything understandable. Don't be afraid to ask questions; he will help you, I promise.

He's the best chem prof i've had. He wants you to pass and will help answer any questions you have. All his assignments are very clear and he truly understands what he is teaching. He's also really easy to talk to and actually helps during office hours. Definitely get what you put into the class

Easily the best professor Ive had! Class material is difficult but he does his absolute best to simplify concepts. He is super passionate about chem and is always willing to help with assignments. Quizzes and exams accurately resemble the lecture activities and exam reviews so you will always know what to expect. I highly recommend his class!

One of my favorite professors I've ever had! Dr. Turner makes it easy to understand chemistry, and he is willing to help students in anyway he can. Labs are a bit tough, but he was always willing to curve anything he had to.

Dr. Turner is honestly the way to go when it comes to taking chemistry at Ohio State. He's always super friendly and will answer any question you have until you fully understand. My only knock on him is that he was pretty bad at getting back to emails, which isn't great but it was manageable considering how helpful he is during recitation hours.
Turner is easily one of Ohio State's best professors. He wants all of his students to pass his class and has many opportunities for extra credit and help with homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Very knowledgeable chemistry professor. He explains in more than one way if you do not understand. 3 Midterms and 1 noncumulative final. Failed at main but received an A- here. Put in effort ask question and you will be amazed in this class.

Dr. Turner is an amazing professor and is very knowledgable. Easy quizzes if you study, tests are harder even though they are multiple choice. The labs are very easy points as long as you attend the lab. He is always available if you need additional help, he will also spend as much time necessary in class to answer everyone's questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Turner is a pretty straight forward guy. If you show up to class, do the hw and chapter definitions you should get an A. Labs are pretty laid back and he's very clear about write up instructions.
Read your book and you'll be okay.
cute and all but he makes me feel like I should already know all this "basic" stuff.
He's a cutie :)

I had him for 121 and 122.He was great.Chemistry is a very difficult subject but he made it about as easy as it could be.He made things very clear.I loved his class.I am very greatful I had him for chemistry!

i had him in 121, 122, 123, he's an awesome teacher, he understands every bit of chemistry and explains it very well.sometimes he doesn't spend much time on the hard stuff, then you have to go to his office for help, and he provides it. his multiple choice tests are very hard, you have to be really prepared.

Bless this mans heart!!! i've never had a nicer teacher he's so sweet and cares about you and your grade. He always open for questions and concerns and will give extensions if you are polite about it. Easiest math class by far as long as you study and do your work.

Professor Maharry is an absolute gem of a professor to have! 
I recently had him for summer Trigonometry, and I will describe my experience:
He was very caring about each and every student, took his time for each and every student, and made sure in his lectures that everyone will understand the concept smoothly.  
Not once has he shown negativity!

He is extremely caring about students retaining information. At no point did I ever feel embarrassed for asking a question (which is rare for me in math classes). He does a great job at coaching students through the problems and is always available for one on one time if needed. Usually, we had weekly homework assignments, four quizzes, and exams.
Really helpful. Attendance is part of grade. Homework online. Gives great review sheets.

An excellent professor, genuinely enjoys the course material and teaching. He will go out of his way to answer any questions and help you learn. Very flexible and understanding. He grades based on the work you do, but isn't hash. Sometimes he goes on tangents or gets distracted and doesn't finish a practice problem or two.
I thought he was a super nice oerson but at times hard to understand, never finished practice problems all the way which was annoying!
Easy going, fun and helpful
Dr. Maharry is the best!  He helps with every problem you have with gentleness and understanding.  He has a genuine interest in students and math.  I highly recommend him.
Dr. Maharry is a really good teacher and makes 116, which is sort of a weird class, understandable and fun.
Great Professor. Funny, witty, and extremely helpful.

If you are bad at writing, I suggest to not take this class if you want an easy A. The 'trick' to getting an A is to attend his online office hours. His lectures are long, but the quizzes are intuitive so you don't have to watch the full lecture. If you go to office hours he tells you what he's looking for in the assignments. Overall great teacher.

You better be ready to read, listen to long lectures, and watch multiple videos every week. Each lecture video is over an hour, you have to read multiple chapters of the textbook, multiple articles, and multiple youtube videos every week

Professor Orcena is chill and very accommodating. The class in itself is very intuitive and easy to follow. However, he can be a pain in getting ahold of via email. Had to wait 1-2 weeks for a response. Otherwise, a decent teacher overall.
Hes a great teacher and very nice!! He gives a good amount of work that is actually interesting and fun to learn about. He is also very understanding.
He reads directly from his slides. It was an interesting class but he was very nervous when speaking which I personally found annoying to deal with.

Professor Jobrack is a great teacher. I took his class online, but he still seemed to care about his students. The Medical Anthropology class he taught was genuinely interesting, and he didn't have a lot of work involved either. I would definitely recommend taking this class.

Dr. Jobrack has got to be about one of the most adorable people I have ever met. You can tell he not only cares passionately about his job but also his students. He trips over chairs sometimes.. a lot but, he just kept talking. He is lecture heavy and his quizzes can be difficult if you don't listen to what he is saying. Overall, great guy.

Professor Jobracks course contains quizzes and discussion posts are very easy to do and you learn quite a lot about them. My only real issue is that on the weekends he does assign quizzes to be done and this would be great if he opened them up at the beginning of the week but unfortunately, they open on Fridays... This includes Thanksgiving break.

Jobrack was a nice professor to have in class but he was also very confusing to follow during his lectures. When I say this I mean that his lectures were unorganized and he went kind of all over the place during them. The material was interesting though so I guess it kept me engaged.

Dr. Jobrack is a great professor who really understands the material, and is willing to help you if you need it. His lectures can be a little disorganized, but if you pay attention and take decent notes, you should do well in his class. He also provides a detailed study guide for each exam, and is willing to allow you to bring up your grade.

I only took this class bc it was 2 hrs instead of 4 hrs of bio for GE, but I wish I took bio. Every class, he wasted at least 10 mins with the same technical difficulty as the last 10 classes. He gave us too much time to complete labs and then got angry when we finished and left early. His lectures were so disjointed I didn't listen half the time.

Dr. Jobrack is a great professor. His classes are a little challenging but he is very knowledgable and willing to help and answer questions. Study for his tests as they can be challenging. But he will provide study guides to his exams and homework is pretty light. Extra credit is also commonly offered which helps. Accessible outside of class.

I really enjoy his class.  Everything was clear and easy to understand.  Professor Jobrack was always available to help with material and gave several opportunities for extra credit in the even t that you missed a lab or paper.  I would take this class again in a heartbeat.
Not organized enough.

Dr. Jobrack is a fair professor and was really good at expalining things and was always there when you needed him. While he was good and all and did offer extra credit you still needed to work for your grade. I went from mildly interested to fascinated during the semester. I managed an A and do recomend Dr. Jobrack as a teacher.

Great Prof. and an even nicer person. He relates makes complex concepts such as evolution, genes, and DNA easy and enjoyable to learn. His homework is relative, and his workload for the students is manageable, but not "easy" by any means. He truly wants his students to learn and enjoy the class. Also does a zoo trip as part of the class, lot of fun

The class was very easy, as long as you read your book and printed off the power point lectures. He strayed from the syllabus a lot, but only because he didn't want to rush us. Overall, as long as you were at least mildly proactive, you would do well.
Hands-down most informative class taken. A lot of bonus point opportunity. Class often defects from syllabus schedule. Grades posted somewhat late. Little else to complain about.

He did not explain anything well like what to do on assignments and gave out a study guide that said the test was multiple choice once but it was a booklet you read then filled out a piece of blank paper with answer.
No comment.

Calling Dr. Lang a good professor is an understatement. Very helpful, cares about students, and enthusiastic about mathematics. I really believe those who struggle with math can benefit from having him as he makes sure you understand concepts and always verifies that you understand. Visit him during his office hours if you are struggling.

Professor Lang is by far my favorite professor Ive had. He does a really great job at teaching the information and he makes sure to really focus on the difficult things. He gives way too many second chances (not complaining though) and if you really take in his feedback after quizzes, youll do great
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Lang was the best professor that I ever had! He was always willing to help and asked students individually, in class, if they understood the material and if they needed help! He always went around the room using every chalk board and was very enthusiastic when it came to teaching! He told many math-related jokes and kept the class happy!

I didn't do great because the material is challenging, without Dr. Lang I would have failed, no doubt. He gets so into his lectures you forget you're in school and you just enjoy it. He is very lenient on some things, he gives out study guides, and you can tell he actually cares. 11/10, phenomenal.

He's a really caring professor that gets into his job. He will give extra credit and retakes if needed. Will meet with you after class on any occasion. Also is flexible if you cannot make it to class the day of a quiz or exam!
He's very passionate about teaching and is always willing to meet with you after class if you have any questions.

Always willing to assist you with any question you might have. The problems in which he executes in class as well as the problems given for homework line up with with the quizzes and tests he gives. Overall an amazing professor with an even better accent. 10 times out of 10 would recommend.

Dr. Lang is amazing! He is so helpful and engaging, I feel that he really wants to teach us the info vs just doing his job. He bends over backwards to explain things to you in different ways if you don't get it. He is so flexible on his office hrs, he will not give up on you. He makes a special point to pronounce his words so you can understand him

Dr. Lang is such a wonderful professor. He goes out of his way to help you if you are struggling. He gives you a lot of leg room on homework as long as you are trying. He is not difficult to understand, he pronounces his words very efficiently. He is by far my favorite professor, I wish I had him in high school.
He is very helpful, and if you  struggle with math- but he sees you try (go ask him questions ect.) he will keep it in mind when grading. Hands down, best professor I've ever had.
Really good teacher. Made math class enjoyable.

Professor Lang is hands-down the best Math professor I have ever had. In high school, I did not like Math at all and when I took 1131 with Jan Lang, he made me enjoy it. He explains things very clearly and is always available for office hours. He will explain something as many times as he needs to, for you to understand- he will even demonstrate.
I am awful at math. Calc was really hard for me and I struggled a lot. I'm just happy he passed me.

Very nice professor knows whats and have an interesting accent. He will always try to explain to you what you don't understand. Very easy and fair grading....I took all my 130s level and got a B-, A-, and A in 130, 131, and 132 math respectively.. Make sure you write whatever he writes on the board and never skip the class...I recommend taking this if you want learn math and got good grade.....!

Dr. Lang is an amazing professor. At first, you may think that you won't be able to understand through his accent, but he makes sure everyone understands. He truly cares about his students and will meet with you whenever you need help.

Absolutely terrible teacher. Very messy and jumps from chalkboard to chalkboard so you have no clue where the equation starts. He is very entertaining when it comes to loving his job, he even jumps on tables and desks, but I taught myself straight out of the book the whole quarter. Very difficult to understand but he is always there to help you.
Agreed he is a great Math professor only one I have ever had that made math somewhat fun.I think he is a wonderful teacher and truly wants us all to do well.

He is a good professor. Like everyone said, he is really excited about math. His quizzes are easy but the exams are pretty hard cuz he will not give you any formulas, so u have to learn them all. Sit infront of the class cuz you wont be able to hear him if u r at the very back..Overall, really nice guy..take his class! :-)

He's a great teacher! At first, you think you're not going to understand him, but you'll get used to it. He's SO enthusiastic about math & he's always making big motions with his hands & jumping across the room. It's what makes his class so fun. He's a very helpful teacher overall & i'd recommend him anyday to anyone.

I have never seen someone so excited about Math before. All the jumping and hes so happy with it all. I think thats what makes his class not so bad.(This is coming from someone who hates math) Him being so enthusiastic makes you not so  bored. He is also always willing to help and LOVES answering questions and giving examples.

Great Prof. ever. He is very willing to help you, and if you do his homeworks and test samples, you are automaticly guaranteed a good grade. He gets so excited about math and he calls problems a beautiful princess and he jumps around class. Highly recommended.
IT IS HARD TO TELL ABOUT THIS PROFESSER. HE IS FINE BUT , HIS EXAM IS CONFUSING. BUT IF YOU GOOD ON MATH NOT EXPECTED TO MUCH FROM HIM, YOU WILL BE FINE.

Hilarious!!! he just jumps around the class and uses a lot of arm motions.  his jokes are not all that funny which is what makes them funny.  great guy and great teacher.  Great for a small classroom.

Very hard to understand, doesn't seem to care much about helping you, and doesn't take control of his class.  He let two kids in my class continually talk during the entire class period, and it was very hard to focus.
He is sometimes hard to understand.  He is a great guy though!  Very helpful in and out of class.

The class was a little hard but he was always there to explain the problems you need help with.  He has a czech accent but it just makes you pay more attention; he also is very careful with his words.  I got a B+ in his class, when in high school i always had C's.  Great Prof.

Can be difficult at times, you may need to come in outside of class time, but the professor is great! A really fun class atmosphere where your encouraged to talk and work with you peers in productive ways.
great professor but can be strict at times.

reviews.comment: The class is an easy A as long as you participate and turn in your assignments. This course added very little to my knowledge of art, but was an easy A toward meeting graduation requirements.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He is very unclear and for a beginning level drawing class, it is very intense
Hes a good professor
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He is awful at communicating. Never sends out emails like he says he will. Finals week never sent anything out what needs to be turned in or when it needs turned in. I dont not recommend this class.
Unorganised, unfair, I'm a 39.7 GPA and he gave me my first d.

Scot was a thorn in my side for 2 quarters. But after confronting him about it, he was much less cranky. I certainly am a much better artist than I used to be before taking his class. He does a great job communicating expectations. If you expect to get a grade on every assignment, you won't. He mostly grades on overall improvement. Great Teacher!

He definitely does not hand out grades, you earn them. Very harsh but you'll be a better artist after taking a class from him. More than half of the students in my my class dropped it after the first two weeks. Assignments were very time consuming and not much time to do them. Since the university is in semesters now, that should be better.
I have the class on MWF and he showed up on Friday once. Love the class!
Harsh, but very true. Amazing at what he does!
Dude is the real deal. Stop hating- all these negative comments? You must be terrible artists. Get a life.
Needs to get over himself!

Honestly i was really excited to take this class, but i had no idea what she wanted in terms of the writing criteria. The feedback given barely helped and i was left confused.The timeline for grading is atrocious you don't know if your failing or not. I also got very mixed responses that didn't help.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Taking her Anthropology class. Jesus why is it so hard to understand what she wants out of us. Half of the work is on a separate platform apart from canvas and the reminders to do them are so ambiguous.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took this class as an elective. Hardest class I've ever taken. A very reading heavy class, you have to make sure you are carefully reading everything. Took too long to grade and I was left stressed with a C- almost the whole semester. Reflection essays were left without a rubric. It was hard to tell exactly what she wanted. I learned a lot though
Thinks her filler class is main character and thinks a 3 credit hour class should take 30 hours of reading a week.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Rask is a very good Professor. I opted to take another class with her after Classical Mythology.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Assigning assignments on Thursday night to be due on Sunday. Long prompts for reflection essay that are hard to understand what she wants out of you. So many readings and videos to watch. D not take her courses with a lot of other classes because you will have to dedicate every night and weekend to completing her assignments, that are worthless.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She requires an incredible amount of difficult reading. The amount of projects and homework for a 3 hour GE is not worth it. I have taken 2 classes with her and she routinely changes assignments with less than 24 hours notice which require hours to complete.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Rask (contrary to popular belief) is a PHENOMENAL professor. Yes, her classes may be rather difficult but, she is always there for support. If you don't want to work hard, do not take this class, easy. It was challenging but, one of those most impactful classes I have ever taken and learned from. She is caring, understanding, and resourceful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Rask "teaches" by demanding you to read at home and repeating like a parrot "Everything we have covered is fair game on the quiz/test" like it is helpful. If you like to read mythology you will be fine. But if you have no time to read, you will fail because the slides are worthless, and if you skip class to read, she'll fail you for skipping.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dr. Rask is an excellent professor. I've already taken two of her classes for fun. The material she teaches is interesting but pay attention to what she's saying, NOT what is on the PowerPoint. You'll always have access to what is on the PP after class. She expects a lot of complicated reading but thoroughly talks about it in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This class was definitely a lot harder than I thought it was going to be. Rask was very nice but the material was very complex and I don't think she did a good job of explaining it. There was also a lot of quizzes with a very small length of time on each of them. There are definitely better history professors to get a history GEC from.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Idk how her normal in-person classes are but, I won't be taking another one of her classes again. She has timed tests and quizzes, which normally isn't a big deal. But her quizzes are set to 25 minutes and can have up to 40 questions and her tests are set to 60 minutes with up to 95 questions. There's sometimes a curve if you're lucky.

Ms. Rask is a very smart, energetic, and interesting young professor who will force you to expand your mind and think critically!  If I hadn't taken all of her classes I could, I would take more from her just for the experience!

Took the online version of 2001, and it was not hard at all. As long as you stay on top of your work you're pretty much guaranteed a B. Exams and quizzes are weighted heavily but are easy if you study and are curved. Responds to emails pretty fast and the class is organized well.
Great teacher, would take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The class was structured well, not too much homework, lectured based. Gives curve and extra credit.
Doesn't know how to teach.
Dr. Khatri is a good instructor. He is well organized and gives good lectures. Attendance is not mandatory, but coming to class will get you extra points.

The class is a bit difficult but he posted all the lectures online which helps. He goes into good depth in his lectures. Also does IClicker which kinda makes you need to show up but you can do it at home if you have your app open

Do not, I repeat do not take this class, while dr. klochko is a really nice fun teacher, her class is terrible, so much lecturing and she gets off topic so much, almost impossible to know what's gonna be on the exams, whole class grade is based on the 3 exams so if u get a bad grade on one of them you screwed for the semester. Please don't take!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Klochko was a great professor. She had lots of slideshows which clearly showed the material. She also had many opportunities to raise your grade through quizzes and assignments on carmen. As long as you do the reading, attend lectures, and study for quizzes and exams, you should do just fine. Exams were large but not difficult.
I survived this class bc of the textbook. Her lectures are very confusing and she is a very tough grader/ no room to make ANY mistakes. Doesn't give good feedback.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Took this class during Covid-19 times and you REALLY have to work hard for a good grade. There are at least 2 short papers each week, which she expects you to discuss during Zoom meetings. She doesn't give rubrics, just gives examples of previous student papers. You can only miss like 2 classes without tanking your participation grade.

She is a pretty laid back prof. Everything you need for the exams are in the notes you should be taking. She does give home, but it is pretty easy. She is a tough grader when it comes to it, but it's just common sense. She can be stern when she needs to, but if you follow her rules, which are pretty insignificant, you'll do great.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She's very fair. I got away with calling Karl Marx and idiot and still got an A or A-. Don't be a child and you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor klochko is cool and all but her accent makes it hard to understand during lecture, considering she talks very fast. Class is mostly lecture

Definitely not "the greatest thing since slice bread", if you know what I mean. If you miss one day in that class, you are setting yourself up for failure, especially when she doesn't do any make-up assignments. Her grading style is difficult to understand and unfair. Good luck if you take this class, you're going to need it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She's not personal, almost to a harsh point. The class as a whole was interesting but the way she goes about teaching it is awful, the textbook is clearer than she ever was.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Klochko is an unpopular Sociology professor at OSU Marion. She has a cold personality. I received As & Bs on almost every test and paper, but missed a few classes because I was sick. She was puzzled on why I was doing well in her class, but somehow I ended up with a C as a final grade. I have received As in all my other Soc classes.

Dr. Klochko is knowledgeable and willing to help students. Though with that being said, she can come off as intimidating. Her expectations for the papers and presentation were not clear and the atmosphere of the classroom was uncomfortable. Don't take this course unless you love to participate and read.
Expectations are very UNclear! Very negative attitude and always harsh.

Dr. Klochko is a great professor, but definitely not a professor you want to take unless you are willing to put forth your best effort. She is extremely demanding, and expects 110% from her students. I learned a great deal from her class, and in the end, though I recived the lowest grade of my college career, I know I got the grade I earned.

Dr. Klochko is a straight-up professor. She doesn't cut any slack and she demands hard work. She expects class participation and grades each student on his or her involvement with the subjects being discussed. If you read the assigned coursework (not really optional), do your homework, and participate in class discussions, then you should do well.

Marianna Klochko is straight up - listen, study and participate and you will do very well. Ignore this and you might as well go home. I have noticed that the complainers don't participate in class and are generally poorly prepared to be a student at OSU. Eventhough Marianna offers help and advice the complainers ignore her open and frequent offers.
EVIL is not a harsh enough word. You don't feel like your in class but more like in some sort of a military academy. And her Ukranian accent makes it hard to understand her.
she is a very mean teacher, be careful if you have feelings, she is evil

Marianna is a wonderful professor. You can count on her to follow through on everything that she says. She is honest and hard working, and expects the same from you. I learned so much from her, and hope to have her again!
Great instructor. Knows her stuff, is direct & to the point. Material & her lectures are the same way.  Really enjoyed this class.  I highly recommen her.
Excellent teacher - would take more courses under her.

This professor is very strict but a grrreat instructor! She definitely knows her stuff and teaches as such. Her exams are tough but if you study and use the guide she hands out, you should do fine. DO NOT talk in class while she is speaking...she will not tolerate! She gives extra credit & encourages office visits. I just love her...cute accent!

She's a nice woman.  Her class comes directly from the book.  I mean DIRECTLY!  Easy exams.  Just make sure you follow exactally what she wants for papers.  She gets really mad when the papers aren't just as she asks them to be.

she was new when i got her, so we took a lot of notes in class, but she knew the material, she wasn't boring and as long as you show up and do the work, you'll get a good grade.  plus she has a hot accent.

I like this professor but sometimes what she says is way beyond my league.  This could probably be contributed to her way out there intelligence level.  Try a google search on her sometime and see if YOU understand what she is talking about :)
A very strict teacher, she will expect you to listen to her talk instead of writing down the notes.  Do not miss classes, you will not catch up, and study the exam reviews throughly.

The class too much work for the credit hours. He clearly knows a lot about his fields, but needing to know the ins and outs of plant reproduction and rote memorization of a bunch of terms that he rattles off in class makes it difficult to retain everything. The field trips are cool the reports are incredibly time consuming on top of the studying.

Dr. Klips is very knowledgeable with plants, but this proves to be an issue as he wants everyone to know everything and over-teaches. Memorization heavy. Quizzes every week over specific information and not just concepts, making it difficult to study. Way too much work for a 2 credit hour, 2000 level class that's held in the first session.

Dr. Klips struggles to communicate what he knows to students. He information dumps and expects everyone to keep up. I can't tell if he is rude or just oblivious in our interactions sometimes. This class is memorization-heavy with a high workload. I like plants and was excited to take it, but it is too much work for an intro class.

This is a 2 credit class, but it took up more time than my 5 credit STEM classes. He seems to be unaware that people take classes other than his. His weekly quizzes are v inconsistent in terms of format and material, so it is impossible to know what to study. Huge amount of work outside of class. That being said, he loves what he teaches
