This is a 2 credit class and he truly believes it is a 5 credit hour class. There are quizzes every week and each quiz is about 4 pages front.. back, and fill in the blank. You basically have to memorize every single word he has ever said and be obsessed with plants. There also are 2 required weekend field trips that you have to go to. AVOID
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Absent minded is the best way to describe him. His classes may be the only ones where its a disadvantage to take at OSUM.
It was such a rough class... You teach yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: After reading other like-minded reviews, it's SHOCKING that OSU continues to employ him. He's confrontational an rude when asked questions for clarification. In my opinion, OSU is assuming a legal liability by employing a professor who creepily takes photographs of students without permission. What does he do with those photos? *Shudder*

Dr. Klipps knows his stuff about Biology but talks so fast and when you ask a question he acts as if you're stupid. All of his quizzes are taken every week and have about 10 pages worth. If you're wanting to pass the class memorization is important; his test are not multiple choice.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: I been at Marion campus for 2 years, this professor was the WORST!

The quizzes we took were pretty easy and straightforward, but the exams we had were not. He waits until the last minute to warn you about exams and goes over the quizzes - but they're over specific things with multiple parts to each question.
He likes to go off topic often and makes a lot of jokes. Study a lot for the tests and quizzes, he's kind of a picky grader.

Dr. Klips is a great biology professor. His homework packets are a little long, but as long as you do them you will do well on the quizzes. He pulls his exam questions right from the quizzes. Dr. Klips is always willing to clarify just go to his office hours. He's not handing out A's just do your work and you will do well!
So rude. Don't dare ask him a question.
I personally liked the way he taught although some people found it hard to follow. He does come off as strange or rude sometimes but I think he is a very nice and helpful professor.

Dr. Klips is a fairly decent professor. He can be challenging at times, and is definitely unusual. I personally find his teaching style refreshing, and it keeps my attention. If you want to do well, pay attention and study. It's commmon sense if you ask me. He's not handing out any A's.
Rude, unhelpful, and bizarre. Who takes pictures of their students at random times during class?

Professor Klipps is a good guy. A little weird at times, but overall a very capable teacher. He is what I expected out of the college experience. No coddling, he is straight up and I respect him for that. You will definitely learn from this class.

I adored Dr. Klips.  He's a bit off the wall of course, but he's amazing and you actually learn things.  Plan on studying though to get a good grade.  He has very common sense moments, so if youd on't ask a stupid question, you're fine.
WOW Ohio State- no standards at all anymore? That's all I have to say.
Professor is RUDE and unwilling to help students. He will not clarify WHAT is going to be on quizzes/exams or WHEN they will take place. Everyone in the class was AFRAID to ask him questions.

Dr. Klipps is one of the most unhelpful professors I have ever encountered. He constantly asks if anyone in the class has questions, but he asks it in a way that seems more like he is "daring" you to ask a question. Whenever the few who are brave enough do confront him with a question, he most often refuses to answer them and degrades them openly.
Such an awesome guy! He is very thorough with his material, but you ACTUALLY learn. His quizzes are sometimes difficult, but with a bit of studying and attention a good grade will come your way.

Dr. Klips is a great professor! All you have to do is go to class, and he writes up all his notes, so you just have to copy it down. The labs were kind of fun, because you don't have to do a lab report like most other science classes, and he tells you at the end of the lab how the results are supposed to be like (How nice!!. Overall he is AWESOME!!

Dr. Klips is without a doubt the worst teacher I have ever had. The man is so absent-minded that it is hard to follow him during lectures. His "quizzes" are absolutely ridiculous, getting an A in this class requires a tremendous amount of effort. He is not helpful at all considering he ignores student e-mails. Be warned!

Plan on putting in a lot of study time to get a good grade. Tons of material to remember for the quizzes and test. Don't miss any classes there is a lot of notes that you are tested on.  Has no sympathy if you have to miss class even with a doctors excuse, but very willing to stay after class to clarify material.
Awesome teacher, he's fun, and still gets the information across.

This guy is nice, but NUTS. He gives you a "quiz" every Friday worth 25 pts. His "quizes" range from 4- 8 pgs and are always diagrams short answers & essays. In addition to the weekly quiz there is a HW assignment worth 5 points that is normally about 10- 15 pgs of the same material for the quiz. Lastly the Final= DEATH! 200 of the same types of ?s

Dr. Parsons is a great teacher and person. Though her Literature adolescents class is a lot of work. You are expected to read one book a week and have a final paper and presentation. Basically the only grades is a 10 point test a week so if you don't do good on all of them your grade will suffer and she never gives out 100% This class is doable
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Absolutely incredible professor. Look forward to having her again!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: I feel as if I may have gotten the most out of this class than any other this semester.  If you're looking to become the best teacher that you can become, the experience and skill of Dr. Parsons is something that you'll want to utilize as much as possible.  I feel very lucky to have been her student and I hope to take another of her classes in the

Such an amazing professor and person. I had true growth as a writer in his class and feel privileged to have taken it. He provides a safe space and genuinely cares about his students. If you have an opportunity to take one of Stuarts classes, I would highly recommend going for it!

Professor Lishan is a really nice guy and he is very very good at English. But at times I felt like I had to be as good in English as him to get an A in that class. Really great guy, just grades really hard. However while taking the class my English skills have gotten so much better
Lishan is super nice and pretty laid back.  As long as you do the assignments (and there are quite a few, but they're pretty easy) you should get a good grade.
No Comments
Class is tough but very interesting. Teacher keeps class fun yet intellectual. Be prepared to work hard, keep up on readings and assignments, and revise to get A's and B's.
Took the class as a GE credit. Definitely a tough class if youre not interested in politics.

She's a great person but her quizzes are impossible. The class is already 3 hours long and then you have to go home and read books and 40 page long articles. Even if you do the reading, the quizzes will still seem difficult. If you get something wrong, she makes you feel dumb in front of the whole class.
would not take again. not clear on instructions

This class is NOT taught as a beginners course. Be prepared to completely teach yourself throughout the whole course, as well as teach yourself entire sections over night. Not clear or consistent when emailing. You may wait up to a week for a response, & sometimes will never get one. Utilize the few extra credit opportunities & the learning center.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Mansouri is a great professor. He teaches his students in a way that he wishes he was taught in college. He really tries to explain the concepts, but sometimes he may be a little hard to follow if you fall behind, so staying engaged and asking questions is important.

Absolutly sucked, Had him for ENGRTEC 1600. One of the wose classes I've ever taken not a hard course by any means but the teaching on the other had was awful. Didn't explain a thing and then when it came to grading it would take forever and give bad grades even if you followed directions to a tee. Would STAY AWAY if possible.
Good professor that knows what he is talking about.

The instructor provides minimal guidance and does not assist students who struggle with assignments. Quiz questions are poorly worded and difficult to comprehend. Assignments are graded weeks later, making it impossible to correct earlier mistakes in a timely manner. I do not recommend taking beginner classes with this instructor.

The class can literally become an online class! He insists that you show up for the lectures or else he'll drop you from the class, but you don't really learn anything from him. He assigns everything (other than quizzes) on zybooks and the only other aspect is the quizzes. You basically have to study everything on your own.

I would advise against taking CSE 1222; I'm not a CSE person, but this class is required for my major, so I had to take it. The lectures made me feel constantly behind, despite attending every lecture and studying all the material, which was an immense amount of work. Additionally, the tests are absolutely awful everything is in Zybooks.

CSE1223-All content is on Zybook, I barely went to lecture and still had a great grasp of Java concepts. It felt more like a self-taught class. I was able to move at my own pace and finish assignments early since you can read ahead and work on labs/projects early. quizzes were in person--and he graded a bit hard on them.

I took CSE 1223 with professor Mansouri, and it was an overall good experience. I had no prior coding experience before taking this class, but I was still able to do very well. He provides super helpful examples in lectures, mostly based on an assigned textbook reading from the previous week. Quizzes are open note and generally pretty easy also.

Currently Taking Nima's CSE 1223 (Java) class at OSU - main campus.  He is more familiar with C languages than Java, but he still teaches the concepts well. I often ask him questions after class and he helps me figure out the most efficient way to solve problems. He allows make-up quizzes, but he has a good nose for BS. Would take his class again.

This will probably be the hardest class that I have ever taken in college and I haven't even graduated yet. Going in I had little to no experience with coding and after having to drop out of the class I would say that coding is not my thing. The professor was very grouchy during class time and would not ever explain the material more than once.

He does the exact opposite of what the zybooks company recommends for their coding textbook and assignments. They encourage working together, in groups, in order to figure out coding, its not easy. He allows no help ever from anyone and you will be caught. Even on a 20 point homework assignment. Exams are worth way too much of the final grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Great prof. Cares for students. I forgot a HW once and he went against policy weeks later to give me half credit. He also went at great lengths so I could retake a quiz I totally forgot about and even gave me full credit! Class requires effort, but you should be fine if you are working to understand the material.

Dr. B made an accelerated fast-paced course not only tolerable but easy. He spends a lot of time on the basic concepts that you will need to apply to harder questions. Accessible outside and during class, and goes above and beyond for his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: He can sometimes be hard to understand, but this class is pretty easy so if you just read through the lecture slides you will be fine. He makes sure everybody is understanding the course and will help you with any question you have, even during labs and quizzes.
Avoid him and this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I got a C overall in the course but even I realize Marc is an amazing professor, you either like him or hate him there is no in between. I am personally taking the second required accounting course at CSCC, simply because 78% of your final grade is exam weight. In short, if you are good at math, this course should be alright.

Professor Smith was the most difficult and least understanding professor I had during my time at OSU. Attendance is absolutely mandatory, and there are no excused absences for any reason including death of a family member, or having COVID. And the exams are most of the class grade, and you will not be prepared though any provided materials.

Each week there are a series of 5-7 videos that are 5-10 minutes long each and that is all the lectures material for the week. It is vital that you do ALL the extra practice problems to succeed even though they are not required. If you go to all the lectures and do all the homework, this class is a walk in the park A.

Extremely difficult class. The exams are either get the correct answer or get it wrong no credit for work. He also does not give you any formulas you have to memorize them all. The quizzes he gives are also difficult although you do get to work with everyone on them if you want. The main issue with the quizzes are the way he words things.

I don't enjoy accounting, but Marc Smith was a great professor. The lectures were informative and organized, but trust me when I say you NEED to do the practice problems even if they're not required. For the 1st and 2nd midterm, I just re-read lecture notes, got a C on both exams. For the final, I did every practice problem assigned and got 102%.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: test
One word... "awesome"

An outstanding instructor- probably the best in the accounting department and the state of Ohio!. Yes his exams are hard, but he really has a passion for teaching and explains the ideas and concepts very well. To do well in his class- practice as much as you can and take down as much of the notes as much as possible.
Highly recommend Marc. The class isn't easy, but you're going to learn and retain the information better in his class compared to anyone else.
An enjoyable professor to have, but he is a tough grader

very clear, awesome teacher...he should run the lab portion of the class.  he explains everythign in detail, is  very interested in helping.  go over the problems he has on carmen over, and over, and over again to do well on exams.  puts bonues questions on exams.  there is NO CURVE in this class.  class average was a 66%, no CURVE...weed-out class

Marc is the best professor I have ever had at OSU. He teaches in such a fashion that is comprehendable to anyone who pays attention. His tests are a bit on the difficult side but you usually know exactly what to study to do well. He was such an awesome professor that if I wasn't graduating next quarter that I would change my major to accounting.
Amazing.  He made this a fun class for me.  You have to go to class, but all the tests and quizes come right from his notes.

Marc is a great teacher...the class was very difficult but it's accounting ...you can't expect it to be easy.  He is so enthusiastic and knowledgeable..you won't want to miss a class.  Second exam seems to be the hardest....study a lot and dont buy the book, its only good for the examples because his notes are so detailed and all on the board.
I think this class was hard.  First C in my whole college career so far and I'm a sophomore.
Tough tests, quizzes relatively easy. make SURE you PRACTICE THE EXAMPLE PROBLEMS!!!!
Difficult tests, but great teacher.
Very tough prof, but really explains things well.  I actually enjoyed going to class - its well worth your time!  Take him - he's tough, but fair and nice and you'll learn ALOT!
She is such a sweet human and cares about your success!

Patty is the best professor you could ask for!! She cares so much about you and your success. She is a fair grader and loves when you ask questions, she wants you to know the material your learning. She loves when you interact with the lesson, it makes the class vibe so much fun

I absolutely love Patty! She is so kind and considerate! Her directions on assignments are a little vague at times so I would ask clarifying questions. She loves to answer your questions so don't be afraid to ask!
Patti is wonderful. Lots of homework, but you always know exactly what is expected. Extremely organized.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This class will provide you with information which will serve you well into your career.  The preparation of material is meticulously selected to provide the student with everything they'll need.  Prof. Muir is extremely dedicated to each student doing their best and I'm so glad that I had her for my first semester.

He is one of the best professors on the campus! You can make a good friendship with him even after you move on to more advanced classes! He is such a joy! Swahili is an easy language to learn and he is a great professor for it!

Zach was very nice and funny (he had an African type of humor since he is from Kenya) he lets out early a lot and his class was very easy. We only had to do two essays and it was pretty fun. Very amazing, nice, and caring guy with a much more fun than I expected class
Put in the effort and you will get the grade you deserve.

Attendance is mandatory in all of his classes. The course was very simple and you can work at your own pace. However, joining group discussions in class is very important because that's where %90 of the course material is discussed. Easy A honestly if you put in a decent amount of work.
Amazing professor, would total recommend his classes. Very sweet and funny guy
It was hard for me to stay interested but he chose good books to read and write the papers on.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor Zak to me is an awesome teacher!! His grading criteria is clear as day. We did read a lot but that's expected when you take literacy class. We read three books in class. He gives you one of three choices that's worth 30% of the final grade. 1. Write journals (15 pages) 2. Write two book reports (6 pages each) 3. Research paper (15 pages)
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Zak was incredible. In African Lit, we got to rewrite the ending of one of the novels we read. There were parts in which you might need to include some sort of ritual that wasn't discussed in the novel... I wrote Insert info about xxx ritual here. He liked my ending so well and saw that I "got" it that he put me in touch with the author!

I had professor Nyongesa for Swahili 1101 and it was an awful experience. He gives midterms without any warning and refuses to communicate with his students.  He seems to want to make the class as confusing as possible and has zero structure or reason behind the grades he gives out. If you want to get the grade you deserve do not take this class!

Professor Zak is one of the worst that I've ever had. He teaches well but never reviews his handouts - which end up being his exams. There is no transparency in the grading (good luck getting an A if you try), and he has "TAs" who aren't really TAs and could care less about the class. Avoid like the plague if you value your GPA.

Very friendly and VERY outgoing. If you want a GPA booster take this class. He only taught the first two days, the rest of the time were group discussions, videos, or waiting for the entire class for him to try and start the video. Almost impossible to understand but very funny. Assigns big homeworks but never checks or grades them.
I like him.Very qualified to teach

BEST professor ever!! not only was this class super easy, but he made it like 20x better!!! if he knows that u do the readings and work hard, then he's willing to help u a lot, so go to lectures and do the assigned readings-not a lot at all. and ur guaranteed an A. watched like 5 documentaries in class and SAVE all ur notes till the end!!!

Knowledge is in the mind of the creator. We are only willing to see what we are capable of. We can only learn with an open mind.  Professor Nyongesa is a rare jem. A great teacher changes your perception, a different way of learning, looking at the world, expressing yourself etc. It's not about getting an A, it's learning your capability of existen

Zak was a wonderful professor; he would patiently answer questions and you could not offend him with a respectful question about race or culture. He loved his students and their work and encouraged deep thinking. I wish I had the opportunity to work with him again. As long as you were thinking and writing, he was happy and gave good grades.

Professor Zak is a very outgoing man. He is very hard to understand sometimes. He likes to talk to you IN YOUR FACE (literally). Do your readings. Work is very excessive but is extremely easy to get an A if you read. He grades very loosely and its almost impossible to fail his course.

great professor, easy on attendance, and in class participation. very outgoing teacher who cares weather you understand what he is teaching or not.  taking Zak's class is a breeze basically and easy A or B, and even if you dont get the good grades and miss class allot its easy to walk away with a C. i dont know of anyone who has failed a class.
Terrible would NOT recommend!

Zak started out on fire, but fizzled after 2 wks. Does not turn back all of finished assignments, so you have no way of knowing your status until final grade. He does not keep track of anything. Gives A's to students who turn in nothing and less to students who did it all. Take his class if you like a "crap shoot" Do NOT if U value your grades

Professor was very enthusiatic and personable. Lot of reading, but for a purpose. Assignments seemed to be very big and unreasonable, but he graded very very easy. Exams were take home and explained throughly in class. Highly recommend for easy B or A
HE IS THE BEST PROFESOR. JUST SHOW UP ON HIS CLASS. SHOW HIM U ARE INTERESTED IN HIS CLASS, U WILL GET A GOOD GRADE. I HAD A A- FROM HIS CLASS.
I really learned a lot and enjoyed the class. You can occasionally miss a class without a problem.
Great professor. I've taken 5 classes with him. Really seems to care! Always willing to help students out. He has lots of energy and is really funny. Makes class fun to go to!

This professor is very unclear on almost all of his assignments.  There's so much unnecessary writing, seems like he just assigns it because he doesn't know how to teach it for himself.  Nice guy, but very unclear.

Aside from his Shakespeare class, which is the most difficult class out of all of those he teaches, he is a wonderful professor! If you like writing, he is great to talk with and share your work with! His assigned materials may be hard to understand (he's a Joycean scholar, so that makes sense) and may not be engaging, but he is great!

Be prepared to watch multiple Shakespeare plays and actively participate in class. I found it difficult to keep on top of assignments when he tells you about them almost last minute. He grades things a bit weird and not on a consistent schedule. Lectures either stressed me out or made me want to fall asleep. Interesting information though.

Not understanding at all, he expects you to do everything when its due. I was incredibly sick and unable to do much of anything and he didnt seem to care. Get ready for lots of homework. Watching 3 hour Shakespeare plays and answering loads of lengthy questions. Ps the book is like $80
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I loved this class!  Dr. Wallace is a great professor who tries to make his class as enjoyable, engaging, and fun as possible.  The class does contain a work load at times, but I promise that it does pay off.  I have very rarely taken a class and walked away feeling that I had learned this much.  Would highly recommend taking this class!

Covid is now a thing and I would think he would have been more accommodating, but that wasnt the case. He didnt grade half my stuff until the end of December. I took his class as an elective and it was one of my harder classes. The movies were pretty terrible. Not fun like you think a film class would be. I dont recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Wallace is a very nice professor to have for class. He really cares for his students. There is a lot of writing in his class but besides a midterm paper and a final paper the rest were just one page responses. I would recommend his class if you're up for a lot of writing.

Even as an English major this class was more than I expected, but I learned a lot and Wallace is willing to help you understand and is willing to work with you. Discussion based with questions from the homework. Reading could be complicated, but you always talked about it in class. Nice Professor who is very passionate about what he teaches.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Class was discussion-based which is ok because Wallace is great at getting everyone to contribute without pressure. Get ready for TONS of homework. Usually 20 pages minimum, over long weekends he'll give more like all of Othello. Also, you need to type answers to questions posted on Carmen every night, usually a page or longer. Hard, but rewarding.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: A nice professor but maybe it was just the class, it sucked. Nothing was graded until the very last moment and his quizzes were very detail specific & his exams. LOTS of reading and I mean A LOT. It was a boring class. (taken on Newark Campus)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had him at Newark, not Marion, but great man. He was very helpful in my intro english class, and gave great feedback for essays. He is a nice guy to talk to (especially if you like sports!) and even apologized the few times class wasnt let out early! highly recommend him, you will love his class
If you like chill english classes then take him!! He is such a nice guy and really chill person. I learned many things and enjoyed it everyday.
Was late on grading all my papers. It was an inconvenience not knowing my grade till the last week of school. Be ready to work on your own due to little feedback.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--SO MANY PAPERS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I took Dr. Neel's Comparitive Studies course on science, literature, and technology at the Columbus campus, and it was amazing. Dr. Neel's choices in the works we read or viewed facilitated great discussions. He is very lax about essays and usually will give feedback before the due date if turned in early. Overall, I would highly recommend him.

Mr. Neal is a good teacher but is awful at grading papers and giving you feedback. It's the last week of school and I have only 2 papers entered on black board. We can revise past work but no grade means no feedback and that means I'm left to guess what might be wrong with my papers.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--SO MANY PAPERS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Travis was awesome! He was so enthusiastic about the material and it was evident how much he loved teaching. He made discussions interesting and wasn't too tough when it came to grading.  I would definitely recommend taking his class!

Travis is an incredibly enthusiastic professor who makes every class enjoyable. However, be prepared for someone who is super unorganized. I didn't get my mid term grade back until 5 days before the final. However, he loves helping students and teaching.

Incredibly unorganized. The class seemed to have no direction. Did not receive a single grade back until the last week of school! He is very enthusiastic about the topic, but he will ramble on without actually saying anything. Although I was very interested in the topic, I felt as though this class was a waste of my time.
If you get Travis, then you better be super excited! I was dreading this class when I first signed up and it quickly became my favorite! He is so funny and absolutely knows his stuff.

I took Travis' english class at OSU Columbus. He was absolutely excellent. I am a Bio major and not very into English, but I grew to love his Shakespeare class. He is very helpful, passionate, respectful, and kind. I recommend any possible course offered by Dr. Neel. Treated students with a kindness you don't receive from most teachers. Exceptional

Intro to Shakespeare with Travis is one of my favorite classes I've ever taken!  He's hilarious and I had a great time in his class, I'd take another that he teaches without a second thought. Make sure you do the readings!! (Or atleast read the Sparknotes)

Travis is a Shakespeare NUT, and he makes the subject easy. The only homework is reading the plays, but he goes over them in class and never asks questions about them. Some quizzes, but they're only on Sparknotes info. He's funny, passionate, and charming. I would take a class with him again in a heartbeat.

Great teacher, truly enjoy what he is teaching. Very helpful and somewhat flexible with different things pertaining to the class. I would high recommend this teacher, work hard do the readings, and you can receive a good grade.

If you ever get this professor for class, consider yourself absurdly lucky. He is absolutely passionate about what he is teaching and completely hilarious. He isn't afraid to be blunt about the text and he ties so much to modern culture. I would take a class in watching paint dry if this professor taught it.
He rambles a lot about irrelevant information and his tests are impossible
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I was very apprehensive of taking his class. However after taking Microeconomics with him this semester, I can say he is a excellent professor. He genuinely knows what he is talking about and provides tons of examples. As long as you come to class, study, and pay attention, you'll get an A. He's a funny guy who is very understanding. I got an A!
Boring class but had to pay attention...

Very bright man, enthusiastic about his topic.  I took 2 econ classes from him and waited until he was teaching a stats class to take it from him also.  Very knowledgeable, and clearly conveys the information about his subject.  Students will learn if they pay attention--these are not classes to snooze through. I'd give him an A.
Interesting class. The teacher is a brain (extremely smart). Lays out current events and realistic examples to help you understand the material. I enjoyed the class.
Would not help me and told me to skip a funeral to attend his class. Very rude and disrespectful to all students. Worst class ever!
this guy should never teach period

Please ignore the review below, obviously one of the many idiots I witnessed in this class on a weekly basis. This class is VERY easy if you come to class, take notes and read your book. Even without a book this class would be easy because basically 100% of exam material is from lectures. This course is EXTREMELY relevant and Dr. Newton is great.
HARD!!! it's really hard ot pay attention in class. He is really boring! The exams are SO HARD!!!
Good Econ class- great professor!  Explains everything well and very helpful.
I love this professor!  His material is hard- but all econ sucks!  He explains everything very well and is always open to helping.  He's a very easy going guy and very nice.
His voice kills you and your attention gets lost as soon as he opens his mouth.
Newton is a very outgoing guy, but his exams are hard as hell. He gives a lot of good notes and explains them pretty well. As for his exams, they are really hard.
No Comments
Good prof, tough material.

St. Pierre is a very funn guy with an accent that he makes fun of himself. His exams are pretty tough but he literally have a 16% curve on one of them. He gives pop quizzes and leaves blanks in the slides so attendance is necessary.

This was his first time teaching and undergrad class. The exam questions were extremely specific and required a lot of attention to details in notes taken from class. Study hard and the class is pretty easy. Don't blow it off though.
Great Professor, he really knows how to hold your attention. He is nice and seems very passionate about the subject and is very easy to understand.
There is one weel left in the semester and we still dont have a single grade yet.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Talks a lot. Will talk for around 30-40 minutes each class and go to subjects about different things that don't even relate to what we are doing. She is a very nice person and thorough with explanations, but I didn't learn much of anything in her class. I learned more about film than about reading/writing. Very disappointed.

Mrs. Oakes is an amazing person, but her lectures are extremely boring. She talks forever, and her lectures lack importance. However, her class is extremely easy, and her expectations are not very high.
Super kind and gives good feedbacks on assignments and easy grader. But the class was not structured well, she does not update carmen regularly so due dates are unclear most of the time.

She was a very bad professor. She just kept rambling on the whole class. I still haven't gotten a single grade in this class and there's two weeks left in the semester. She is terrible at using Carmen. Her assignments have no clear instructions and if you ask her to explain something, she just talks and talks but doesn't say anything useful.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I am currently in her english class and she is always so sweet and kind, the day she knew she was giving a long boring lecture she brought us cookies to help brighten the mood. Yes, it does take her longer with technology and getting carmen up at the beginning of the year but she never made things difficult for us and always works to help us!

She cannot use CarmenCanvas at all, and it took her WEEKS to get the class page posted online, so for the entire semester, we were weeks behind schedule. She does nothing but ramble, and is absolutely incapable of staying on-topic, so I had to teach myself from the textbooks/course materials on the Carmen page. Avoid this professor at all costs!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Don't get me wrong, Oakes is a wonderful person. However, she cannot use a computer to save her life. The Carmen page is never up to date, and always tells you to go to tech support for all her projects, and doesn't offer any help. She doesn't reply to emails until 1-3am IF you get a response. She also doesn't grade anything until classes are over.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Absolutely amazing! Responds to emails quickly and genuinely cares about giving you the answer you need. The course was intense, but she made it bearable and was forgiving with due dates. I would definitely take another class with her, she is professional, knowledgable, and caring.

She does not understand any technology and will not grade anything important until after the semester is over so you have no idea what your final grade will be. She doesn't give reminders for assignments and assigns things last minute. Nothing is actually taught she just rambles for the whole class so expect to teach yourself out of the textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She doesn't grade anything and doesn't respond to impoortant emails even after sending you one first. Lied about pushing back a due date due to not giving our grades yet. This class stressed me out and the lack of grades until 4 days before the semester ends doesn't help. I took this for a GE and I regret it. Don't reccomend taking it.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Oakes is probably one of the worst professors Ive had. She has no clue how to use the Carmen site and often times doesnt even post the material she promises. incredibly unorganized and doesnt have a single thing graded, so dont expect to know if youre passing. I had to drop my class with her next semester because I cant handle it again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Semester ends in 2 days and I have yet to have a single grade. Boring beyond belief, waste of time and tuition money. she makes several promises and never follows through
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor Oakes is someone who values honesty and participation. I will say she does not get grades back in a timely manner which is why I did 4 instead of 5 stars. If you take advantage of office hours and talk to her directly about your concerns she always goes out of her way to help. You get out of this class what you put into it.

She only handed back 2 assignments the entire semester! At no point could she inform anyone what their grade was because of her massive stack of ungraded papers. Most importantly, she gave us NO RUBRIC TO THE FINAL PAPER. Even after she CONTINUALLY promised too! The last class she gave us a BRIEF glimpse of what it would be, then never did anything

Have the final for the course tomorrow and have yet to receive my first midterm back. I have no idea where I stand with my grade and she went on for 20 minutes in our last class about students missing the point on the free response portion of the midterm. So far, 50/400 points in the course are accounted for and again the final is tomorrow!
Avoid this professor at all costs. If you can fulfill the same GEC requirement with a different English professor, do it. You'll thank us all on here later!

It feels like I've learned nothing in this class. You listen to a whole lecture then leave thinking what just happened? EVERY. CLASS. Just do yourself a favor and stay away from this professor. We've done maybe 5 assignments in this class and I don't know my grade for any of the assignments and my last day of class is today.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Doesn't teach ANY concepts. Learn to rely on yourself in this class.. if you don't understand the history of popular culture then you better learn before you take this class. She won't teach you. Read from a VERY dense textbook too. So good luck

I took Comparitive Studies and English from Susan, and the only thing we did in class was to discuss our next assignment, that's it! In other words this could have been an online class as we did nothing during class time. Class is extremely hard with a lot of assignments and homework, specially the assignments we got in finals week drained me out!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--SO MANY PAPERS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: She was by far the worst professor I've ever had! She was not clear on what she wanted the class to do and was not organized what so ever. She was also very rude and had a harsh attendance policy. I wouldn't refer this to my worst enemy. STAY AWAY!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Not exactly group projects, but she heavily makes the students work together in groups during class time. A few pop quizzes over material packets and materials from supposed to be read. The major grades are 3 papers (and a final project we haven't gotten to yet this semester) but she gives PLENTY of time and outlines due dates for process material.

Literally the worst professor in existence.  Only graded one of the five papers due, and took two months to get that ONE paper back.  Lectures are boring, very harsh attendance policy.  I have no idea what my grade is.  VERY disorganized.  Assignments are not made clear, and due date are not upheld.  Save yourself some pain, don't take this class.
Don't take this class.

Sue is literally the worst professor i have ever had. We didn't get and grades until the day of the final (one month+ after the test) her assignments are not clearly explained, you basically have to figure out what she expects in her assignments. would not reccommend this class to my worst enemy!

She gives plenty of pointless busy work. I have not learned a thing in the class. Her attendance policy is needlessly harsh, and she automatically fails you if you miss more than 4 classes, unless you bring a doctors note.

She gives a lot of work and a lot of reading. if you do it then you are good but if you don't your screwed. She writes a lot on the borad but almost none of it is important. We had two presentations and two midterms, and one final which was "what have you learned in this course." She does treat it as a 300 level class instead of a 100 level class.

I didnt really like this class at all.It feels like hurry up and wait.The first 2 weeks we had a paper due everyday then did nothing.She says lecture is mandatory and takes away points if you dont go but after lecture I felt there was no point.Constantly doing nothing then suddenly have a big paper or presentation with no preparation.Exhausting.

She treats this class as if it is a 300 level, not a 100. She restates herself and often keeps us late. If you are absent one time without a doctor's note your grade is lowered. This may be the most boring and useless class I've ever taken thus far in college. The entire quarter I had no idea how my grades were because she never handed stuff back..

great prof. you have to do the reading or be a extremely good BSer. really intense the first 5weeks but, settles down toward the end. Cares alot about her students and brings Donatos and a family recipe of bananna bread on the last day of class.

Well, she is kinda friendly but her class is so boring. You have to read all these stupid books to write the required essays and for group works and stuff. The books are super boring no question about that. I think she grades things liberally. Basically, you read the books and take time doing assignments and u get the reward.

Will go above and beyond to help your writing, even if your not in her class. Go to her office hours, she respects that and it will help your grade. I didn't open the book once since I went to all lectures. Subject is so boreing but pretty easy grade.

Omer is a nice guy, and I feel bad that he gets such a bad rap. Giving him the benefit of the doubt, this class is HARD to teach, but he definitely has room for improvement .His lectures weren't really all that clear and useful, and often left me more confused in the end. To anybody taking his STAT 3470 class, use Mark Riser's YouTube Lectures!!!

I thought this class was really easy. The professor was very clear on what would be on the test and you always have practice exams before each exam which are very helpful. If you have to take stats I recommend taking it with him.
Omer was very helpful, and this class was not hard. Do the homework and the practice exams and you'll be fine. Tests are always easier than the exams.

In the beginning of the class, I loved omer. He was fair and thoroughly explained the information. In the last few days of the quarter, he tried to put way too much info into a few classes and everyone ended up doing pretty bad on the exam. Just make sure you do homework

Omer is a very kind man. His tests are very fair and if you do the homework, they are very easy. He expects people to show up and participate but is always available and explains things clearly. Best math teacher I have ever had -- and I hate math!

English is not his first langauge, but his accent wasnt that bad. He really tried to help students understand and didnt get mad when we had to skip whole chapters because we got so far behind from all the questions. He did insist on keeping us for the full 2 hours EVERY CLASS, never got out early once. Overall a good prof though

Likes to give 0's for homework that was completed, Seems to be annoyed when asked questions for clarification. Only makes the class fun at certain times. Take Spanish 3 with a different professor. Overall, don't ask stupid questions and make sure you are always 100% correct or he will think you are incompetent. Tough grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Professor Palomino is great. He really wants his students to do well. He even curved the first exam (which he swore he wouldn't) because he wanted us to be able to succeed later on. He is very accessible outside of class and loves to discuss individual progress. He values participation. His class has a great feel to it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I would avoid taking any of his classes. He acts like he doesn't want to be there and seems annoyed by questions. He encouraged us to ask questions and when we did we were told "we would figure it out later." Disrespectful and rude towards students, tough grader, and downright unpleasant. The tutoring center is helpful, but him, not so much.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Easily the least accessible professor I've ever had at Ohio State. Genuinely does not care how students do in his class, consistently comes to class in bad moods, and does not respect his students. Some activities are fun, like songs, but most of class is spent doing activities taken straight from the book. He obviously does not want to be teaching

He was a solid professor. This class was more difficult than previous Spanish courses, and was not an easy A, but that could also be due to the fact that it was a higher-level Spanish. Lectures consisted mostly of doing practice activities, and while that got tedious at times, it was definitely necessary to practice the many tenses in this course.

Don Teddy is a great man. His class is participation oriented, and as long as you pay attention to due dates and deadlines, you should be fine! Be careful with MyLab because it is worth more than you'd think. Teddy is a super funny guy who really cares about the language, and he is willing to help each student. DON'T BE AFRAID TO ASK HIM QUESTIONS!
The most harsh grader I have ever had as a teacher.

Professor palomino IS AMAZING HANDS DOWN! His teaching style engages the class and helps you understand. He is animated and will help in anyway that he can so that you understand. he grades tough but he will help you find ways to learn it better. The class is fun and i would recommend anyone that wants to better understand Spanish to take it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This course is demanding per the department. Teddy is firm but fair. He does his best to be fair with HW assignments and grading exams and assignments. Participation is IMPORTANT!
He looks tough but if you are polite and address him correctly, he is very nice and helpful. I loved the songs the most. None of my previous instructors gave us songs. Theyre a very cool activity!
I hate Spanish and hated this class. But I respect Dr. Palomino because he listens when you have concerns. Also, he recognizes the problems with the book or the grading systems and takes action.
Professor Palomino prepares you adequately for the exams and whatnot. I got a solid grade. Slow and maybe repetitive pace but not boring.
I thought I would dread this class as I was only taking it because it was required, but to be honest he made it quite bearable. Do all the homework and participate and you will get an A.

I honestly believe Teddy wrote some of the previous reviews himself. I loved my first two semesters of Spanish, but this was a completely different and negative experience. Teddy was not helpful, and did not foster an environment conducive to learning by any means. Did not communicate well with students, and would not recommend.

Teddy only taught in Spanish, and would only speak in English to tell our class that we were terrible at Spanish. His style of teaching was often rude and demeaning towards students, and he seemed to value intimidating his students over helping them.  His forced participation policy didn't help me learn, rather it made me hate attending his class.

I took Teddy's class expecting an excellent final semester of Spanish, but what I got was a neutral experience in the class. I was rather surprised because last semester he got such rave reviews... This complete change in personality could be due to the Spanish department changing the curriculum, but overall, this was my least favorite class.

Teddy was the best professor I had during my first semester at OSU.  He is always willing to meet with students.  Being a native speaker, he is extremely passionate at developing the speaking skills of his students.  Class was always fun, and we learned culture & 10 different Spanish songs.  Please take his class if you have the opportunity.

Teddy's class is a great option if you want to finish GE Spanish with a fun atmosphere. The class follows somewhat of a routine everyday, and Teddy is pretty funny sometimes. This is coming from someone who hated Spanish all through high school. Just do the homework, and learn the culture (actually pretty interesting) and you will be fine.

Teddy is a very enthusiastic, funny, interesting teacher who makes class fun and enjoyable. Participation is a huge component, but it really does help improve your grade and he corrects you in a way that is critical, yet helpful and understanding. I would definitely recommend him to anyone looking into this class!

I love Teddy!!! He is hilarious which makes class a lot of fun. He expects you to participate in class but is very informal about it. Definitely one of my favorite instructors and would recommend him if you have to take Spanish. The class is pretty easy as long as you participate and study for exams. You definitely need the book for homework.
Teddy is a great professor!
Great guy and wonderful professor!

I've taken  several Spanish classes at OSU and had really good professors, but Teddy is the best I had. He knows his subject extremely well. If you take time to talk to him outside of class you will understand how smart he is. He is very approachable and also cute!

Although this was a composition class, I feel like I learned a great deal about culture. Teddy's insights about Latin American reality are really eye-opening. Since he's a native speaker his teaching is focused on what really matters about language. Also he's nice and funny, and very professional as well.
Absolutely the best professor I've ever had. Engages you with material and really cares about your learning. He generates great classroom atmosphere. Always available and answers emails quickly.

Teddy is an awesome teacher. He tells students how a native Spanish-speaker would actually say something as opposed to sticking to the workbook. He is also a really cool person in general, so talk to him about his life if you have a chance.

Teddy is literally the most awesome guy I've ever met. This was the first class in my college career that I actually looked forward to going to everyday. He will really get you involved and enjoying the material. His choice of material is also really interesting. Take him when you can! I hope I can have another class with him.
Teddy was a nice guy, really engaging. It's a good course if you're looking for something challenging, but not so great if you're bad at handling homework load.
Teddy is a great guy! Be prepared to speak in this class, because he randomly calls on students when he asks a question. Easy grader!
Teddy is a great teacher. He is always willing to help and improve your Spanish. He makes class definitely interesting and is really funny and entertaining- he makes learning Spanish fun.
Teddy changed my life! He's unique. I became more fluent because of him. Whenever you need him, he's there to help you. You should take any class he teaches. You won't regret!
Teddy is hilarious and keeps class interesting. He is so chill and still knows the topics extremely well. He knows his stuff. Highly recommend!

Teddy is the reason I decided to second-majoring in Spanish. He truly cares about his students. Always willing to help. Always funny. Approachable like no other and super nice! Any simple topic becomes interesting when he explains it. By way way way far, he’s the best teacher I have ever had, seriously.

Teddy was my favorite Spanish professor yet. He's very laid-back and funny, but still teaches the material very well. Grades pretty leniently, especially on speaking tests. If you go to class every day and study the vocabulary, getting an A isn't difficult.

Teddy is a funny guy. He doesn't speak English too often and says he's bad at English but he is actually not so bad for being in the US for only one year. Because he's a native spanish speaker he can give you some insider tips on the language and make the class a little more entertaining. Grades harder than an english speaker.
Great person, fairly lenient grader. Highly recommend.
Dr. Pettijohn is a big teddy bear. He really cares about his students. His classes are informative and fun. You will learn a lot.
Great guy. Very lenient grader. But you have to know SOMETHING to get points. Take all of his classes. He rocks!
He had a great sense of humor and had great class discussions.
Very nice teacher and helpful overall!  I would definitely recommend him to anyone taking any psychology classes.
fabulous amazing teacher. you know what to expect each and every time you come into the classroom! He is a legend here at OSUM
Wow! Fantastic class! Enjoyed lectures, clear, relevant, interesting. Nice person, caring, kind, considerate.
wow take his class
didnt seem to care about individual students. cared more about just getting his job done
Best prof I have every had in college. Cares about students. Difficult tests, but relevant, and if you do the work, you will do fine in his classes. Great sense of humor. Makes learning fun.

VERY EASY CLASS! Dont buy the book you won't need it if you pay attention in class. 5 short tests not comprehensive midterms or finals VERY EASY, I skipped class like twice a week, never bought the book, and still got an A
Great class!  It's really easy and you actually learn, too!
Very good classes.  Interesting and informative. Very straight forward with material.  Great character!
probably the easiest college class i have ever taken...ever...just go to class and study what he tells you to
Very time consuming but assignments not graded to difficult.
No Comments
No Comments

Professor Tahmina is by far the best professor I have had at Marion in the engineering department. She really cares for your understanding of the material and ensures that by answering questions your questions in class or during her office hours.

Dr. Tahmina knew what we struggled on and would mock us about it. I felt very degraded and disrespected by her. Even when asking for help and showing a want to learn she still mocked us. Ive never felt so disrespected by a professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: Awesome professor. Looking forward to taking her in the future.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If anything, I'd say Dr. Tahmina is too nice. You get all the notes you need for the class and more. She gives helpful reminders and as long as you pay attention, you will do well. I've only taken a couple introduction to coding classes (and forgot 80% of them), which helped a good amount. I definitely appreciate her.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Dr. Tahmina is great, take her! Responds to emails quickly and is always willing to help. You must attend class or you will fall behind though. Lectures can be boring but they are crucial. Graded on 10 online quizzes (easy), 10 pre-labs (pretty easy), and in-class labs (somewhat difficult, but you can always ask for help), and 3 exams (hard).

Dr. Tahmina overall is a good professor. She cares about her students and will make arrangments if something isn't going well with assignments. The class, on the other hand, is not well laid out. OSU wants the students to do a huge workload for just 2 credit hours! I'm taking calculus and physics with a lab and I still do more work here.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Not a bad class, A LOT of homework. You're looking at no less than 4-5 hours of excel/access a week, sometimes upwards of 10 hours in a week. Material is not challenging if you actually pay attention. A lot of people skipped class and sent each other prelabs/worksheets, they didn't do well. Dr. T is kind and caring but her lectures are very boring.

This class is an entry level engineering class. You'll spend the first half learning excel, and second half learning the basics of MATLAB. The teacher was mediocre, but that's because the material she was forced by the university to teach was kind of bland. Just do your homework in the class, and really take your time to learn the MATLAB portion.

Dr. Tahmina's instruction was satisfactory, but the course material created by OSU is very poor. The regular homework and exams were very easy. But the weekly labs could never be finished in time, requiring students to come back later in the week. The lab reports were required to be ridiculously long and did not teach any knowledge to students.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Summarize his lectures in your own words, sometimes he puts a lot of words on his slides. Will give extra credit opportunities if many people ask!
Fun teacher. Really energetic and enthusiastic about course material. Quizzes and tests easy if you pay attention in class and study the powerpoints. Reading assignments were optional and not tested.
Very enthusiastic about forestry and a good teacher.

Dr. Yoder is by far the best professor I have ever had in my college career. You can truly tell how much he loves his job and cares for his students. If you are struggling with content, he is very available outside of class and always willing to help! Ohio State is truly lucky to have him. I could not recommend a professor more than Dr. Yoder!

I LOVED TAKING THIS SUMMER CLASS WITH DR YODER. I was very intimidated by ochem when we started but he does a great job at making the material digestible. He genuinely cares and is very accessible. It was challenging but as long as you watch the lectures and do the problem sets you'll be good. Ton of extra credit opportunities and heavy curve.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Organic chemistry II is a difficult class, however, Dr. Yoder explains the material extremely well and helps students get a good grasp of the material. The course is primarily graded through weekly quizzes and exams. However, this helps immensely understanding concepts. I took the course online, and the class was outlined and explained well.

Yoder makes OCHEM 1 and 2 reasonable. I am thankful that I could take it both semesters with him due to the online format. Way better than the other OSU professors. Reasonable homework and quizzes. The exams step it up, but if you put in the work you can succeed. Good professor.

Honestly, Yoder takes the fear out of OCHEM. He teaches off powerpoint and structures the class around what he will ask on the test. He gives lots of practice problems. Be aware that he does have specific grading criteria, so make sure you understand all aspects of the questions. Quizzes are medium in difficulty and he steps it up for the exams.

Dr. Yoder was an amazing ochem prof. He taught everything clearly. Ochem is Ochem... it's hard, but with Yoder you actually understand the material. I had him for both Ochem 1 and 2. He answeres all your questions and has your best interest in mind. You have to def WORK FOR IT

The lecture is helpful in understanding the upcoming lab. A little bit of a harsh grader when it comes to formal lab reports nearing the end of the semester. Multiple solving for unknown assignments are all-or-nothing and rather challenging.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Very lecture heavy. Extremely recommended you do not skip class, though attendance is not graded. If you pay attention during class you should be fine. Quizzes and exams are geared towards your specific class and what you may struggle with. Class is heavily curved. Very accessible in person and email. Explains things very well.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Amazing professor! He is my role model! He is a great professor that wants kids to pass the class but he gives you all the material you need. Just practice makes perfect!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Metacognition on this guy.  He knows how to teach.  Results oriented professor.  In retrospect not a lot to do but still perceived as so at the time.  Curriculum is outdated and he tries to instruct around that with teamwork in mind.  Quizzes and 2 or 3 exams  grade.  Focus on your goal.  Pace yourself.  Identify if you're improving soon.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Best professor I've had.

Dr.Yoder is absolutely amazing! OSUM was very lucky to get him. He is an excellent instructor and explains the material very well. He is tough but extremely fair. His door is always open and he is more than willing to help his students. He even held study sessions for exams on his own time. He's one of the best teachers I've had. Great job Dr. Y!!!

This professor constantly tells the class that he expects the test averages to be low, but instead of trying to help raise the averages he just lets everyone do poorly. I wouldn't say that this class is hard I would just say that it is taught poorly. When we should have been reviewing material for the final he decided to cover new material.

Dr. Tiff is by far the best professor I have ever encountered in college. Organic chemistry is hard, so you will have to work for your grade, but Tiff made this content significantly easier. She is not only a great professor, but an incredibly caring person. I could not recommend a professor more.

Dr. Tiff is one of the best professors at OSU. She cares a lot about her students and understands that OChem is a difficult subject. She gives bonus before every midterm and the final and is very generous with curves. 11/10 would take Dr. Tiff!

Dr. Rye-McCurdy cares about her students and their success. She takes the time to go over practice problems and questions with students, even if the questions seem to be menial, she still takes the time to make sure students understand the material. Her office hours and STEM Center hours are invaluable and help further content understanding.
Avoid

Zellmer is truly the greatest teacher I have ever had. He is also the hardest. (About one-third of the class dropped after the first exam) He is very rigid but could not be more fair in his grading. Remember that, no matter how much you are working, he is putting in twice as much. If you don't love Dr. Z, re-evaluate your decision to be in school.
If you go into his office hours, he can be a wealth of knowledge, prepare for alot of work!
Cool guy. Very easy to approach, seems intimidating at first but is very helpful.
Amazing highly recommend

Professor Zimmerman is an excellent calculus professor. He explains everything in clear, detailed steps and makes even the harder concepts feel more manageable. He takes his time going through examples, and is always willing to re-explain something if someone is confused. His teaching style really helps build a strong understanding of the material.
Most amazing professor. Understanding and wants you to succeed. He doesn't hide anything in he syllabus adn is upfront with all his students. He is so sweet and caring and deserves the world.

He is a very nice professor. He doesn't make you feel stupid for any questions that you ask. He always leaves time at the end of class if you need help with anything or have anymore questions. He likes to joke around and makes sure you understand the content. He always shows different ways to work the problems so you learn best.

I took his math courses 1125 and 1126. He is a very caring teacher and offers many opportunities for you to display your understanding. He is available for questions and is willing to go more in depth if you need help. He provided useful feedback. I loved his class! Very beneficial class for future teachers!

This course was very difficult the way it was set up. The lecture slides on Canvas were there for you to access but they did not have the steps to show you how to do the problems. Don't expect any curves on any exams. I personally felt like steps were skipped while doing examples in class which caused me some confusion in class.

I took this class to get my Gen Ed requirement and it sounded mid but I think was kind of interesting because of the professor. It was my first semester, and she made it clear and easy to follow. I had to read more than other classes this fall and be prepared there will be work outside of class but it was pretty easy to get a B.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Sawicki is a knowledgeable and gifted lecturer. She has a firm grasp of the subject matter and knows how to present it coherently. I hope I am able to take another one of her classes.

Be ready to feel like you took an english class, there are a ton of essays and extended responses and if you use spell check, to her it is considered cheating. In the class she talks about discrimination and being someone with a learning / spelling disability that requires accommodations I have never felt more discriminated in my life.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I learned a lot from this professor every day, great class! She is amazing.
Amazing professor. Lectures were great and I learned a lot in this class.
This was the best psych class that I have been a part of. V. Sawicki was amazing in all aspects.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: A passionate professor Sawicki was communicative and accommodating when I had a family emergency offering alternative solutions. Great communication! 10/10 would Sawicki again.
Really clear and easy to get a good grade. Basically, just do the work when it's assigned.

The actual class was psych 1375. Her teaching style is fairly lecture heavy, then proceeds with a writing assignment/class discussion. She makes sure everyone understands what she's asking for by providing clear rubrics and examples. Creates a class environment that is comfortable for people to speak up during these discussions. Great professor!
Actual class was Psych 1375: Identity and Culture. Good professor, and took time to make sure we understand the concepts and criteria. Would take again
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Very discriminatory and very rude! She didn't want to accommodate or communicate efficiently or effectively even with my physical disability and Legal Accommodations. Felt very unsafe and uncomfortable but got treated like I was incompetent. I had to withdraw from her class due to emotional distress and anxiety she created. Very Unprofessional!

Dr. Sawicki was one of the best professors I've ever had. Her lectures coupled with her assignment flexibility allowed me to thrive in her course while learning material that I can apply throughout the rest of my life. Dr. S was one of the most fun, caring, and attentive teachers I've ever come across. You will not regret taking this course!

This class was very enjoying and I learned a lot from it. We rarely had homework and honestly, I finished most of the week's work in an hour. Definitely recommend taking ay class you can with this professor.

Do NOT take a class with Sawicki. When I had a family emergency, she essentially told me that I needed to have done the work on time and turn it in on time. Very few chances to get your grade up if you miss a discussion/reflection. Will be petitioning for grade forgiveness and taking class with any other intro professor. AVOID IF ABLE

Sawicki is by far the worst professor I have ever had. She is incredibly rude, and does not provide any detailed feedback, no matter if she claims she did.  She is a horrible grader, she is extremely strict and only gives you the link to the rubric as "feedback". AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!!!!
She is kinda rude and goes back on her work

She doesnt allow any technology in her class room despite the university providing us with iPads and encouraging us to use them. So I everything for my classes on the iPad except for this class. She docked my grade twice for one paper.

The greatest prof. I have ever had. It is clear what she expects from you so if you do what she says, the classes are pretty easy. If you take notes from her power points, there is no reason you shouldn't do well on the exams. She is younger, so I think that she understands the students more. Always willing to help, or just talk if you want.

She's very specific on what she expects in the papers. If you follow along with the rubrics given, you will do well. Proofread your papers SEVERAL times. The papers are not long, but she doesn't like what she calls "word fat." Exams are written, but she provides a study guide and those were helpful. If you go to class and study, you will do fine.

Not many points available, only 200 for the whole Social Psych class, yet a tough grader and she even said "you won't get 100% on some papers". No extra credit available. I found myself struggling to get every point I could.

I too am an untraditional student, and I really enjoy Vanessa's class. You know what to expect and where you stand. Put the required work in and you'll be fine. She challenges you in hopes that you will learn something. She is really nice and offers help if needed through email, office hours or after class.

Vanessa is great, she spells out the expectations in the beginning, if you aren't up to it, drop. Class and lectures are enjoyable, be prepared to be challenged and actually learn something. Very aproachable, wants your patricipsation and feedback. As untraditional student, I find her class enjoyable! Notes are clear, tells you what to write down.

This prof has unrealistic demands and expectations. I wonder if she realizes we are full time students. She is picky and sensitive about everything. Between quizzes after EVERY chapter and writing 7 papers, and one big paper and the midterms--which you have to take meticulous notes because she makes it clear she won't provide you with any. Run!

Three quizzes, three exams. Final exam is noncumulative. Very direct and to the point. Randomly passes around a sign-in sheet to gauge attendance. NOTE: If you're under 18, you have to write EIGHT summaries of research papers to get research credit (10% of grade). If you're over 18, you get to participate in experiments for that credit instead.

Professor Tylka is a very sweet and caring professor, and her lectures are super simple-just notes from a PowerPoint-but are long. I would highly recommend her course to other freshmen at Marion, especially if you want an easy A!
Lectures are in depth and interesting, gives points for everything, quizzes and tests are based directly off of notes and homework. My favorite prof this semester

A very kind professor. Lectures were interesting, as she didn't read off slides word for word and added insight from what she has learned, or personal experiences. A textbook was not necessary, but an option was given if you needed extra help. Would definitely take her classes again.

Hands down THE best professor here at OSU Marion. Her class was the highlight of my semester, and as long as you do your work and study, you'll do amazing in this class. I have never met such a well-respected, kind, and charismatic professor before! I truly wish her all the best. Thank you for everything, Dr. Tylka!
Dr. Tylka is an amazing professor. She definitely cares about your performance in class and will do almost anything to help you be better and understand more. I would 100% take her class again!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Show up and listen to what she says and you pass. Her review sheets are spot on with her exams. If you can answer the questions on the review sheets your golden, also her exams are very simple and easy to understand all you have to do is just study.

Loved Dr. Tylka, she makes you work for your grade but provides plenty of feedback and help if you need it. Her lectures were intresting, she gives you full notes and has you fill in the blanks. She's a very nice person, I like her area of study. Enjoyed her class a lot!

Dr. Tylka is absolutely one of the best professors I've ever had. Her expectations are clear, she's more than happy to help, and her experience in the field really shines through. She's a very sweet lady, too! I strongly recommend using the study guides that she provides.

Dr. Tylka is extremely nice and down to Earth. Her lectures are interesting, and she provides powerpoint notes for you to print and bring to class with you. She re-reads definitions three times, so you don't miss a thing. She doesn't over-complicate things either. She doesn't take attendance, but if you don't go to class you'll miss a lot.

Dr. Tylka is a very nice and laid back teacher. She explains things clearly and provides her power points online to take notes. Not too much from the textbook. She provides study guides for her tests so you know exactly what to expect on her tests. I recommend taking a class with Dr. Tylka.

I loved this class. I've taken a few classes with Dr. Tylka and I've enjoyed them all. She doesn't require that you come to class, but if you don't you will miss a lot, especially because we did not use a textbook. You can get an A in the class if you come to class, take the notes, and read over them.
Really good instructor, super interesting and very clear in expectations!
Very nice, clear lectures, extremely helpful and took time to help anyone who needed it. By far one of my best teachers.
Excellent professor! Very organized and equally kind.
She was a great teacher!
She is seriously an awesome teacher
good prof
I have taken one class with her psych 100 and I will not hesitate to take another with her, she is one of the best teachers I have had.
GREAT prof!!!  She is so interesting and helpful.  The exams are easy if you study and she gives you review sheets as well as optional review class time.  I loved her!

A really awesome instructor!!! I am a junior majoring in Psych and will take every class I can with Tracy. She makes class fun and genuinely cares about her students.She adds a little personality to her lectures with excerpts from her life. I LOVE her dearly!  A+++++ professor!

Dr.Tylka DEFINITELY knows her stuff! Have taken more than one class with her and she is just totally awesome! She repeats herself 3 times during note taking so there is no way you can't keep up. EASY notes and interesting, fun lectures. She is EXTREMELY willing to help you succeed in her classes!
One of the best professors at OSU. Her class is very well organized. Strongly recommend her class.

She is uninterested is an understatement. One thing comes to mind, those who can't do teach, with her I get where the phrase comes from. IDC about her family and life I care about what I am paying to be taught so do it or go be a stay at home mommy.
This is an online course, so expect to teach yourself. She is completely uninterested in helping you. If you fall behind then you need to be sure to get back on it.

You have to show up to class or you WILL fail the exams. If you are a weak note taker, your best bet is to record the lecture & write them out later. His lectures are very fast paced & he can randomly get off track or have to back track because he mixed something up. I never opened the textbook once. The exams are purely from the lectures.

He's a great professor who is really passionate about his work. His lectures are a bit long, but so detailed and concise that I honestly didn't even need the textbook; just my notes. If you take notes and pay attention, you will do well in his class. He's also very approachable, and willing to answer any questions.

Worst class I have ever taken, he lectures the entire class and expects you to remember about everything he says. Tests are brutal, he wants you to remember around 35 buildings and dates of each. Very unclear guidelines for grading, and extremely outdated class style. This class is torture along with the professor. Do not take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I worked my tail off in his class and only got a B-. He must have total silence while lecturing or he will be rude to you. Even if you rustle papers or drop your pencil, he'll call you out in the middle of class. Kinda rude. Used profanity a few times. Not very professional. Class itself is easy. It's just memorizing stuff. Outdated teaching style.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Great professor, awful class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: McNiven is a great professor and very passionate with everything he does. However, there are only 3 grades the whole semester, which are 3 exams.
